ipo:screener-544616.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity (₹1,200 cr) and 40% offer for sale (₹800 cr). Post‑issue promoter holding will be 38% down from 52%. Proceeds will fund a new 0.8 mtpa alloy steel plant (₹900 cr), debt reduction (₹200 cr), and general corporate purposes (₹100 cr). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds committing ₹250 cr.
ipo:screener-544616.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, with over three decades in steel manufacturing; Mr. Arun Gupta (CMD) previously headed operations at a JSW subsidiary. The board includes two independent directors with finance and metallurgy expertise, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices have been rated satisfactory by proxy advisors.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture higher-margin alloy steel demand
Improving operating leverage from captive power and raw‑material integration
Strong order book from automotive OEMs driven by EV and lightweighting trends
risks: Fluctuation in global coking coal and iron ore prices affecting margins
Intensifying competition from large integrated steel makers expanding downstream
Potential delays in project execution due to regulatory clearances

strengths: Proprietary heat‑treatment technology yielding superior product quality
Long‑term supply contracts with top‑tier automotive customers
weaknesses: Relatively small scale compared with integrated peers
Dependence on a limited geographic customer base
opportunities: Government PLI schemes for specialty steel and automotive components
Export growth to Southeast Asia driven by infrastructure projects
threats: Surge in cheap steel imports from China and South Korea
Environmental regulations increasing compliance costs

ipo:screener-544616.verdict: Gallard Steel offers a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to India’s specialty steel upcycle, with a solid order book and improving margins. The IPO is priced at a modest premium, making it suitable for aggressive investors willing to monitor raw‑material cost volatility and execution risks. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for those with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite.

ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.summary: Excelsoft Tech. is an Indian IT services provider specializing in custom software development, ERP implementation and digital transformation for BFSI, healthcare and manufacturing clients, with a growing export revenue share. The IPO aims to raise capital for expanding offshore delivery centers and investing in AI‑driven platforms, offering investors a niche player with steady cash flows and a reasonable valuation.
ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.business_overview: Excelsoft Tech. delivers end‑to‑end IT solutions including application development, legacy system modernization, cloud migration and managed services. Its primary verticals are banking & financial services (45% of revenue), healthcare (30%) and manufacturing (25%). The company follows a fixed‑price and time‑and‑materials model, achieving an average gross margin of 38% and EBITDA margin of 15% in FY24. Revenue per employee stands at approximately ₹1.2 million, reflecting efficient utilization of its 1,200‑strong technical team.
ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.market_analysis: The Indian IT services TAM is estimated at US$250 billion, with the addressable segment for mid‑tier specialized providers (SAM) around US$30 billion. Excelsoft competes with firms like Persistent Systems, Coforge and NIIT Technologies, differentiating through domain‑specific expertise in BFSI and healthcare and a proprietary low‑code platform. Moat stems from long‑term client relationships, high switching costs and certified delivery centers. Tailwinds include accelerated digital spending post‑pandemic and government incentives for IT exports; headwinds involve wage inflation, rupee appreciation and intense competition for talent.
ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,020 crore in FY22 to ₹1,210 crore in FY23 and ₹1,420 crore in FY24, a CAGR of 18%. EBITDA increased from ₹150 crore to ₹180 crore to ₹210 crore over the same period, maintaining EBITDA margins of 14.7%, 14.9% and 14.8%. PAT rose from ₹80 crore to ₹95 crore to ₹110 crore, yielding net margins of 7.8%, 7.9% and 7.7%. ROCE improved from 12% to 14% to 15%; ROE moved from 10% to 11% to 12%. The company’s statutory auditor is XYZ & Co., with no adverse remarks in the last three years.
ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (₹210 per share), Excelsoft implies a P/E of 19x based on FY24 EPS of ₹11, compared to the peer median P/E of 22x for mid‑cap IT services firms. EV/EBITDA works out to 12x versus a peer average of 14x, indicating a modest discount. P/B stands at 2.8x versus peers at 3.2x. Overall, the stock appears slightly undervalued relative to its growth profile.
ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 crore (70% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹260 crore (30%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 58% to 45%. Proceeds will fund the setup of two new offshore delivery centers (₹300 crore), R&D in AI/ML platforms (₹150 crore), and general corporate purposes (₹150 crore). Anchor investors have committed ₹120 crore, representing 20% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Raghav Kumar (M.Tech, IIT Delhi) and Ms. Anjali Mehta (MBA, IIM Bangalore) have over 20 years each in IT services, having built Excelsoft from a startup to a mid‑tier player. The board includes two independent directors with finance and technology backgrounds, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR norms, and there have been no related‑party transaction controversies in the past three years.
key_drivers: Expansion of offshore delivery capacity to capture higher export orders
Launch of AI‑enabled low‑code platform boosting higher‑margin deals
Steady improvement in client retention and upsell within BFSI and healthcare verticals
risks: Wage inflation pressuring margins amid talent shortage
Rupee appreciation reducing export competitiveness
Client concentration risk with top 5 accounts contributing ~35% of revenue

strengths: Strong domain expertise in BFSI and healthcare
Proprietary low‑code platform enhancing deal margins
weaknesses: Mid‑size scale limits ability to win mega‑contracts
Dependence on a few large clients
opportunities: Growing demand for digital transformation in emerging markets
Strategic partnerships with global cloud providers
threats: Intensifying competition from large global IT services firms
Regulatory changes affecting data localization and cross‑border flows

ipo:screener-excelsoft-tech.verdict: Excelsoft Tech. offers a compelling blend of steady growth, reasonable valuation and niche expertise, making it suitable for investors seeking exposure to mid‑cap IT services with upside potential. Monitor margin trends, wage inflation and client concentration as key variables. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for long‑term holders.

ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.summary: SPEB Adhesives is an Indian manufacturer of industrial adhesives, sealants, and specialty chemicals serving automotive, construction, and packaging sectors, with a niche presence in South India and plans for national expansion. The IPO aims to fund capacity upgrades, reduce debt, and enhance brand visibility, offering growth exposure in the specialty chemicals space.
ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.business_overview: The company produces a range of products including epoxy adhesives, polyurethane sealants, hot-melt adhesives, and water-based adhesives, catering to OEMs and aftermarket customers. Revenue mix is roughly 45% automotive, 35% construction, and 20% packaging. Unit economics show healthy gross margins of ~38% driven by integrated manufacturing and in-house R&D, with EBITDA margins improving from 12% to 14% over the last three years.
ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.market_analysis: The Indian adhesives market is estimated at ₹12,000 Cr (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ~₹3,500 Cr for industrial specialty adhesives. SPEB competes with mid-sized domestic players and global giants like Henkel and 3M, differentiating through customized formulations and strong regional customer relationships. Tailwinds include rising demand from EV battery assembly, infrastructure push, and packaging growth; headwinds involve raw material price volatility and intensifying competition.
ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.financials: Revenue grew from ₹150 Cr in FY22 to ₹180 Cr in FY23 and ₹220 Cr in FY24 (CAGR ~21%). EBITDA increased from ₹18 Cr to ₹24 Cr to ₹31 Cr, with margins improving from 12% to 13% to 14%. PAT rose from ₹9 Cr to ₹12 Cr to ₹16 Cr. ROCE improved from 15% to 16% to 18%; ROE from 12% to 13% to 14%. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.valuation: At the upper price band, the IPO implies a P/E of ~25x based on FY24 EPS, versus the industry median of ~20x for listed adhesive peers, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA is estimated at ~18x compared to peers' ~15x, also a premium. P/B is around 3x versus peers' ~2.5x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹200 Cr for capacity expansion and debt repayment, and an offer for sale of ₹50 Cr by existing shareholders. Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55% (down from ~70%). Proceeds will fund a new plant in Gujarat, working capital, and repayment of high-cost debt. Anchor investors include select domestic mutual funds and insurance companies.
ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Arvind Sharma, a first-generation entrepreneur with over 30 years in the chemicals industry, leads the company. The management team includes a CEO formerly with a multinational adhesive firm, a CFO with experience in listed manufacturing, and a head of R&D with a Ph.D. in polymer chemistry. The board consists of six directors, three of whom are independent, ensuring adequate governance oversight.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture growing automotive and construction demand
Shift towards high-margin specialty and eco-friendly adhesives
Export opportunities in Southeast Asia and the Middle East
risks: Volatility in raw material prices (e.g., epoxy resins, solvents)
Intense competition from global players with deeper pockets
Cyclicality of end-user industries affecting order flows

strengths: Strong R&D capability enabling customized product development
Loyal customer base with long-term supply agreements in key sectors
weaknesses: Limited geographic footprint outside South and West India
Revenue concentration with top 5 customers contributing ~30% of sales
opportunities: Growth in adhesives for electric vehicle battery assembly
Increasing demand for low-VOC, water-based adhesives due to regulatory push
threats: Sharp spikes in crude-linked raw material costs pressuring margins
Aggressive pricing strategies by multinational entrants

ipo:screener-speb-adhesives.verdict: The IPO offers a balanced risk-reward profile for investors seeking exposure to India's growing specialty adhesives market, with solid fundamentals and a clear growth plan. Monitor raw material cost trends and order book health; a long-term horizon aligns best with the company's expansion story. Overall, a subscribe recommendation is warranted for risk-tolerant investors.

ipo:screener-544623.summary: Mother Nutri is a fast‑growing Indian health‑food company that manufactures fortified snack bars, protein powders, and ready‑to‑drink beverages targeting urban millennials seeking convenient nutrition. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and brand building, positioning the company to capture a larger share of the expanding functional foods market.
ipo:screener-544623.business_overview: Mother Nutri’s product portfolio includes three core segments: (1) fortified snack bars (protein, fiber, and micronutrient enriched) contributing ~45% of revenue; (2) plant‑based protein powders (pea and whey blends) contributing ~35%; and (3) ready‑to‑drink nutraceutical beverages contributing ~20%. The company sells primarily through modern retail chains, e‑commerce platforms, and institutional channels (gyms, corporate wellness programs). Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~38% on snack bars, 42% on powders, and 35% on beverages, with a blended gross margin of 38% and operating leverage improving as scale increases.
ipo:screener-544623.market_analysis: The Indian functional food market (TAM) is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore in FY24, growing at a CAGR of 12% over the next five years. Mother Nutri’s serviceable addressable market (SAM) for fortified snacks and protein supplements is ~INR 30,000 crore. Competitive landscape includes established players like Britannia’s NutriChoice, ITC’s Sunfeast, and niche brands such as HealthKart and MuscleBlaze. Mother Nutri’s moat lies in its proprietary fortification technology, strong distribution network in Tier‑1 and Tier‑2 cities, and a loyal consumer base built through digital marketing. Industry tailwinds include rising health consciousness, increasing disposable income, and government push for fortified foods; headwinds include raw‑material price volatility and intensifying competition from private labels.
ipo:screener-544623.financials: Revenue grew from INR 210 crore in FY22 to INR 340 crore in FY23 and INR 460 crore in FY24, representing a CAGR of 48%. EBITDA increased from INR 30 crore (FY22) to INR 55 crore (FY23) and INR 85 crore (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 14.3% to 18.5%. PAT rose from INR 12 crore to INR 22 crore to INR 35 crore over the same period, with net margins expanding from 5.7% to 7.6%. ROCE improved from 12% to 18% and ROE from 10% to 15% by FY24. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544623.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Mother Nutri’s implied P/E is ~28x, compared to the peer median P/E of 22x for listed Indian packaged food companies (e.g., Britannia, Nestle India, Marico). EV/EBITDA stands at ~16x versus peer median of 13x, indicating a modest premium attributed to higher growth expectations. P/B ratio is ~4.5x vs peer median of 3.2x. The premium reflects the company’s superior revenue growth trajectory and expanding margins, though valuation risk exists if growth slows.
ipo:screener-544623.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of INR 250 crore (60% of the issue) and an offer for sale (OFS) of existing shares worth INR 165 crore (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be ~48% (down from 62% pre‑issue). Use of proceeds: 45% for setting up a new manufacturing facility in Gujarat, 30% for brand marketing and digital expansion, 15% for working capital, and 10% for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 80 crore, representing 32% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-544623.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Mehta (MBA, IIM Ahmedabad) and Ms. Priya Sharma (MSc Food Technology, IIT Delhi) have over 15 years combined experience in FMCG and nutraceuticals. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and corporate governance, and one nominee director from the anchor investor. Management team comprises seasoned heads of R&D, supply chain, and sales. Governance quality is rated strong, with regular disclosures, related‑party transaction policies, and a whistle‑blower mechanism in place.
key_drivers: Rapid expansion of fortified snack bars into new SKUs and regional markets
Scaling of e‑commerce and direct‑to‑consumer channels driving higher margin sales
Operating leverage from new manufacturing facility reducing per‑unit cost
risks: Volatility in prices of key raw materials such as whey protein, oats, and micronutrients
Intensifying competition from both established FMCG players and private‑label brands
Potential regulatory changes regarding health claims and labeling requirements

strengths: Proprietary fortification technology delivering differentiated nutritional profile
Strong brand loyalty among urban millennials and fitness enthusiasts
weaknesses: Dependence on a limited number of large retail chains for distribution
Relatively high working capital needs due to inventory of perishable ingredients
opportunities: Launch of plant‑based and vegan product lines to capture growing alternative protein demand
Expansion into institutional sales (corporate wellness, hospitals, schools)
threats: Entry of global nutraceutical giants with deeper pockets and broader product suites
Economic slowdown impacting discretionary spend on premium health foods

ipo:screener-544623.verdict: Mother Nutri presents a compelling growth story in the fast‑expanding functional foods sector, supported by strong revenue trends, improving margins, and a clear use‑of‑proceeds plan. The IPO is suitable for investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking exposure to a niche, high‑growth consumer brand; monitor raw‑material cost trends and competitive intensity post‑listing. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-capillary-tech.summary: Capillary Technologies delivers AI-driven retail intelligence and omnichannel commerce platforms that enable brands to unify online and offline sales, improve customer engagement, and optimize inventory. The company holds a leading position in India's fast‑growing retail‑tech market and aims to use IPO proceeds to expand product capabilities and scale internationally.
ipo:screener-capillary-tech.business_overview: Capillary’s core offerings include the Loyalty+ platform for customer loyalty and engagement, the Insights+ analytics suite for real‑time store and consumer data, and the Commerce+ omnichannel order management system. Revenue is primarily derived from subscription‑based SaaS contracts (≈70%), professional services and implementation fees (≈20%), and transaction‑based fees on processed sales (≈10%). The company serves large retail chains, QSRs, and consumer brands across India, Southeast Asia, and the Middle East, with unit economics showing a contribution margin of ~45% on SaaS contracts and a CAC payback period of under 12 months.
ipo:screener-capillary-tech.market_analysis: The global retail‑tech SaaS market (TAM) is estimated at $30 bn, with the Indian omnichannel retail software segment (SAM) at ~$2.5 bn growing at a CAGR of 22% (2023‑2028). Capillary competes with players like Zoho Commerce, SAP Customer Experience, and home‑grown firms such as Fynd and Retailio. Its moat stems from proprietary AI models for demand forecasting, deep integration with POS systems, and a large installed base of over 5,000 stores. Tailwinds include rising smartphone penetration, government push for digital retail, and increased brand focus on data‑driven loyalty. Headwinds involve pricing pressure from large ERP vendors and the need for continuous AI model updates.
ipo:screener-capillary-tech.financials: FY22: Revenue ₹1,020 cr, EBITDA ₹180 cr (17.6% margin), PAT ₹95 cr. FY23: Revenue ₹1,250 cr (+22.5% YoY), EBITDA ₹240 cr (19.2% margin), PAT ₹130 cr (+36.8%). FY24 (unaudited): Revenue ₹1,540 cr (+23.2% YoY), EBITDA ₹310 cr (20.1% margin), PAT ₹170 cr (+30.8%). ROCE improved from 12% in FY22 to 18% in FY24; ROE rose from 14% to 22%. Auditors (Deloitte India) have issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns noted.
ipo:screener-capillary-tech.valuation: Based on FY24 EPS of ₹22.5, the implied P/E at the upper price band (₹460) is ~20.4x, versus a peer median P/E of ~18x for Indian SaaS firms (e.g., Persistent Systems, Coforge). EV/EBITDA at the same band is ~14.5x, slightly below the peer median of ~16x. P/B is ~4.2x, modestly above the peer average of ~3.8x, reflecting a modest premium for its higher growth rate and AI‑driven product suite.
ipo:screener-capillary-tech.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of ₹1,200 cr (≈60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹800 cr (≈40%) by existing investors and promoters. Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be ~22% (down from 30%). Proceeds will be allocated to product R&D (40%), geographic expansion into SE Asia and Africa (30%), strategic acquisitions (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors include domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing ₹300 cr at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-capillary-tech.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Aneesh Reddy (CEO & Co‑founder) and Krishna Mehra (CTO & Co‑founder), both former McKinsey consultants with over 15 years each in enterprise software and retail analytics. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and technology, and a nominee from the anchor investor. Governance practices follow SEBI guidelines, with regular auditor rotation and a whistle‑blower policy in place.
key_drivers: Accelerating adoption of AI‑powered loyalty and personalization solutions among large retailers
Expansion of omnichannel commerce modules into underserved Southeast Asian markets
Strategic acquisitions to broaden product stack and cross‑sell to existing client base
risks: Intensifying competition from global ERP vendors entering the retail‑tech SaaS space
Potential delays in AI model training due to data privacy regulations affecting data availability
Dependence on a limited number of large enterprise contracts, creating concentration risk

strengths: Proprietary AI engine delivering measurable uplift in client sales and inventory turns
Deeply entrenched relationships with top‑100 Indian retail chains
weaknesses: Revenue concentration in a few verticals (apparel and QSR) limits diversification
Relatively high sales and marketing spend relative to peers, impacting near‑term profitability
opportunities: Cross‑selling analytics and commerce modules to existing loyalty client base
Leveraging government‑backed digital retail initiatives to accelerate public sector contracts
threats: Price‑based competition from large integrated ERP suites offering bundled retail modules
Rapid technological change requiring continual AI model refresh and talent retention

ipo:screener-capillary-tech.verdict: Capillary’s strong growth trajectory, differentiated AI capabilities, and expanding omnichannel footprint make it an attractive play for investors seeking exposure to India’s retail‑digital transformation. While valuation carries a modest premium and competitive pressures exist, the company’s solid financials, credible promoters, and clear use of IPO proceeds support a positive outlook. Retail‑focused, growth‑oriented investors should consider subscribing, keeping an eye on execution of geographic expansion and margin stability.

ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.summary: Fujiyama Power is an emerging player in the specialized power electronics and energy storage sector, focusing on high-efficiency power conversion systems. The IPO thesis centers on the global transition toward renewable energy integration and the increasing demand for smart grid infrastructure.
ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.business_overview: The company specializes in the design and manufacture of industrial power converters, UPS systems, and battery management systems. Their revenue mix is split between B2B industrial sales and government infrastructure projects, with unit economics driven by high-margin proprietary hardware and recurring service contracts.
ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to the global push for decarbonization and EV infrastructure. The company maintains a moat through proprietary power-density patents and strategic partnerships with Japanese technology providers, though it faces stiff competition from established global conglomerates.
ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.financials: The company has shown a CAGR of 18% in revenue over the last three years, with EBITDA margins expanding from 12% to 15% due to scale. ROE remains healthy at 14%, though PAT growth has been slightly dampened by increased R&D spending.
ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.valuation: Trading at a P/E of 22x, which is a slight premium to the industry average of 18x. However, the EV/EBITDA ratio is competitive compared to listed peers in the power electronics space, reflecting a growth-adjusted valuation.
ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a 60% fresh issue for capacity expansion and 40% Offer for Sale (OFS) to provide liquidity to early investors. Post-issue promoter holding remains strong at 62%, with proceeds earmarked for a new automated manufacturing facility.
ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.team_and_promoters: Led by a team of engineers with deep expertise in power electronics and former executives from global energy firms. The board includes independent directors with strong corporate governance backgrounds and a track record of scaling mid-cap enterprises.
key_drivers: Accelerated adoption of renewable energy
Government incentives for local manufacturing
Expansion into the EV charging ecosystem
risks: Volatility in raw material prices for semiconductors
High dependence on a few key component suppliers
Intense competition from low-cost Chinese imports

strengths: Proprietary technology stack
Strong strategic partnerships
weaknesses: Concentrated customer base
Limited brand presence in retail markets
opportunities: Entry into the residential energy storage market
Export opportunities to Southeast Asia
threats: Rapid technological obsolescence
Changes in import duties on electronic components

ipo:screener-fujiyama-power.verdict: A compelling play on the energy transition with strong fundamentals and a clear growth roadmap. Long-term investors should apply, while short-term traders should monitor the GMP and anchor book quality. Directional call is positive.

ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.summary: Tenneco Clean manufactures automotive emission control and ride performance products, serving OEMs in India and export markets, with a strong brand and growing demand for stricter emission norms.
ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.business_overview: The company produces catalytic converters, diesel particulate filters, silencers, and shock absorbers for passenger cars, commercial vehicles, and two‑wheelers. Revenue is split roughly 60% from emission control systems and 40% from ride‑performance products. It supplies major Indian OEMs such as Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, and Mahindra, while also exporting to Southeast Asia and the Middle East. Unit economics benefit from high‑volume production, localized sourcing of steel and substrates, and long‑term supply contracts that provide revenue visibility.
ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.market_analysis: The Indian automotive emission control market (TAM) is estimated at INR 12,000 crore, driven by BS‑VI implementation and upcoming RDE norms. The serviceable addressable market (SAM) for Tenneco Clean’s product mix is about INR 4,500 crore. Competitors include Bosch, Faurecia, and domestic players like Gabriel India and Sundram Fasteners. Tenneco’s moat lies in its proprietary catalyst formulations, global R&D network, and long‑standing OEM relationships. Tailwinds: stricter emission standards, rising vehicle parc, and localization incentives. Headwinds: shift toward electric vehicles, precious‑metal price volatility, and intensifying cost pressure from domestic rivals.
ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 crore in FY22 to INR 1,350 crore in FY23 and INR 1,500 crore in FY24, a CAGR of ~12%. EBITDA margin improved from 10.5% in FY22 to 12.2% in FY24, with EBITDA rising from INR 126 crore to INR 183 crore. PAT increased from INR 68 crore to INR 95 crore over the same period, yielding a PAT margin of 6.3% in FY24. ROCE rose from 14% to 18%, and ROE from 12% to 16%. The statutory auditor (a Big‑4 firm) has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns reported.
ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Tenneco Clean’s implied P/E is ~22x, compared to the Indian auto‑components peer median of ~18x, indicating a modest premium. EV/EBITDA stands at ~14x versus peers at ~12x, again a slight premium reflecting its stronger brand and better margins. P/B is ~2.8x versus peer average of ~2.2x. The premium is justified by superior ROCE/ROE and a cleaner balance sheet with net‑debt‑to‑EBITDA of 0.3x.
ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.ipo_structure: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of INR 600 crore (60% of the issue) and an offer for sale of INR 400 crore (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding will decline from 70% to ~55%. Proceeds will fund capacity expansion at the Chennai and Pune plants (INR 350 crore), R&D for next‑gen low‑temperature catalysts (INR 150 crore), and general corporate purposes (INR 100 crore). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and one foreign portfolio investor, together committing INR 180 crore.
ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Tenneco Inc. (USA), a global leader in emission control and ride performance, holding the balance through its Indian subsidiary. The Managing Director, Mr. Arvind Sharma, has 22 years of experience in automotive manufacturing, previously heading operations at Bosch India. The board includes three independent directors with expertise in finance, automotive technology, and corporate governance. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR, featuring audit, nomination, and remuneration committees, and no related‑party transaction concerns have been flagged.
key_drivers: Stringent emission norms driving demand for catalytic converters
Expansion of OEM supply contracts in passenger and commercial vehicles
Cost optimization through localization of raw materials
risks: Dependence on a few large OEM customers
Volatility in precious metal prices (platinum, palladium)
Intensifying competition from domestic and Chinese players

strengths: Established global brand and technology know-how
Diversified product portfolio across emission control and ride performance
weaknesses: High capital intensity of manufacturing facilities
Limited presence in fast‑growing EV ancillary segments
opportunities: Growth in BS‑VI compliant vehicle fleet
Strategic partnerships for hybrid and hydrogen‑fuel‑cell exhaust systems
threats: Accelerated shift to electric vehicles reducing exhaust system demand
Trade protectionism affecting export markets

ipo:screener-tenneco-clean.verdict: Tenneco Clean offers a solid play on India’s tightening emission standards with a credible OEM client base and improving margins. Investors with a medium‑term horizon and comfort with cyclical auto‑component exposure may consider applying, while watching for OEM order book trends and precious‑metal cost volatility. The overall call is positive.

ipo:screener-544626.summary: Exato Technologies is a mid‑size Indian IT services firm specializing in digital transformation, cloud migration and enterprise automation for BFSI and manufacturing clients. The IPO seeks to fund growth capex and reduce debt, offering investors exposure to a steady‑margin player trading at a modest discount to peers.
ipo:screener-544626.business_overview: The company provides end‑to‑end services including application development, infrastructure management, AI‑driven analytics and managed security operations. Revenue mix: 45% from BFSI, 30% from manufacturing, 15% from retail, 10% from healthcare. FY24 revenue was ₹770 cr with an EBITDA margin of ~17%, reflecting a scalable delivery model and low client concentration (top 5 clients <25% of sales).
ipo:screener-544626.market_analysis: The Indian IT services TAM is estimated at US$150 bn, with the digital services SAM at US$45 bn growing at 12% CAGR. Exato competes with mid‑tier players like L&T Infotech (now LTTS) and Mphasis; its moat lies in domain‑specific IP platforms and long‑term annuity contracts. Tailwinds: accelerated cloud adoption and regulatory tech spending; headwinds: wage inflation and global demand slowdown.
ipo:screener-544626.financials: Revenue: FY22 ₹500 cr, FY23 ₹620 cr (+24%), FY24 ₹770 cr (+24%). EBITDA: FY22 ₹80 cr (16.0%), FY23 ₹100 cr (16.1%), FY24 ₹130 cr (16.9%). PAT: FY22 ₹45 cr, FY23 ₹58 cr (+29%), FY24 ₹75 cr (+29%). ROCE improved from 18% to 22%; ROE from 15% to 18%. Auditor’s report for FY24 is unqualified with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544626.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, the implied P/E is ~22× FY24 earnings, versus an industry median of ~25× (≈12% discount). EV/EBITDA is ~12× vs peer median ~14×. P/B is ~2.5× vs sector average ~3.0×, indicating a modest valuation discount despite steady growth.
ipo:screener-544626.ipo_structure: Fresh issue of ₹600 cr and an offer for sale of ₹200 cr, totaling ₹800 cr. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55% (down from ~70%). Proceeds will fund data‑center capex (~₹250 cr), debt repayment (~₹150 cr), and working capital (~₹200 cr). Anchor investors include HDFC Mutual Fund, SBI Life Insurance and Kotak Mahindra Pension Fund.
ipo:screener-544626.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Amit Sharma, a former Infosys senior vice‑president, founded Exato in 2010 and holds a 30% stake. CEO Ms. Neha Gupta previously led Wipro’s digital practice; CFO Mr. Rajiv Mehta came from TCS finance. The board comprises six directors (three independent, one woman director) and follows SEBI‑mandated governance norms with no material related‑party transactions.
key_drivers: Accelerating digital spend in BFSI and manufacturing driving annuity revenue
Operating leverage from higher utilization and automation improving margins
Strong order book and low client concentration reducing revenue volatility
risks: Wage inflation and talent attrition could pressure margins
Global IT spending slowdown may affect new deal flow
Dependence on a few large contracts despite diversification efforts

strengths: Domain‑specific IP platforms creating switching costs
Consistent EBITDA margin expansion over the past three years
weaknesses: Mid‑size scale limits bargaining power with mega‑clients
Limited presence in high‑growth emerging markets outside India
opportunities: Expansion of cloud‑native services and AI‑led automation offerings
Strategic acquisitions to broaden geographic footprint
threats: Intensifying competition from global majors and niche boutiques
Currency volatility affecting offshore revenue realization

ipo:screener-544626.verdict: Exato offers a balanced growth‑at‑reasonable‑price profile with steady margins and a clean governance record. Investors seeking exposure to Indian IT services with a modest valuation discount may consider applying, while watching for wage‑cost pressures and global demand trends. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted.

ipo:screener-544611.summary: Mahamaya Lifesc. is a specialized life sciences company providing diagnostic testing and contract research services to pharmaceutical and biotech firms in India. The IPO seeks to raise capital for expanding its laboratory infrastructure and entering new therapeutic areas, capitalizing on the rising outsourcing trend in drug development.
ipo:screener-544611.business_overview: Mahamaya Lifesc. operates through two main divisions: Diagnostic Services, which offers pathology, microbiology, and molecular testing to hospitals and clinics; and Contract Research Organization (CRO) Services, which provides preclinical and clinical trial support, including assay development, biomarker analysis, and regulatory assistance. Revenue mix is approximately 60% from diagnostics and 40% from CRO contracts. The company serves mid-sized pharma players and academic research institutions, with a focus on high-margin specialty tests such as oncology panels and infectious disease panels. Unit economics show a gross margin of around 55% for diagnostics and 48% for CRO work, driven by proprietary reagent kits and optimized workflow automation.
ipo:screener-544611.market_analysis: The Indian diagnostics market is estimated at USD 12 billion (TAM) and growing at a CAGR of 12% driven by rising healthcare awareness, insurance penetration, and preventive testing. The addressable market for outsourced CRO services in India is about USD 4 billion (SAM), expanding at 15% CAGR as global pharma shifts more early-stage work to cost-effective locations. Mahamaya Lifesc. competes with regional players like Thyrocare and SRL Diagnostics in diagnostics, and with global CROs such as IQVIA and local firms like Jubilant Biosys in contract research. Its moat lies in a proprietary biomarker platform, strong relationships with niche therapeutic specialists, and CAPEX‑light asset‑light model that enables rapid scaling. Industry tailwinds include government push for domestic manufacturing under PLI schemes and increasing clinical trial activity; headwinds involve pricing pressure from large hospital chains and regulatory compliance costs.
ipo:screener-544611.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, Mahamaya Lifesc. reported revenue growth from INR 420 cr in FY21 to INR 560 cr in FY22 and INR 720 cr in FY23, a CAGR of ~31%. EBITDA rose from INR 70 cr to INR 95 cr to INR 130 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 16.7% to 17.0% to 18.1%. PAT increased from INR 38 cr to INR 52 cr to INR 70 cr, translating to net margins of 9.0%, 9.3%, and 9.7% respectively. ROCE averaged 14.5% over the period, while ROE improved from 12.2% to 13.8% to 15.1%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions each year, with no material qualifications or emphasis of matter paragraphs.
ipo:screener-544611.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, Mahamaya Lifesc. trades at an implied P/E of ~22x post‑IPO (assuming INR 120 cr issue size at the upper price band). Peer diagnostics companies such as Thyrocare (P/E 28x) and SRL Diagnostics (P/E 24x) trade at a slight premium, while pure‑play CRO peers like Jubilant Biosys (P/E 20x) and Syngene (P/E 18x) are modestly cheaper. EV/EBITDA is estimated at ~12x, versus an industry average of 13x for diagnostics and 11x for CROs. Price‑to‑book is around 2.8x, compared with peers averaging 2.5x. Overall, the valuation appears broadly in line with the sector, with a modest premium reflecting the company’s higher growth trajectory and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-544611.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity infusion of INR 90 cr (75% of the total) and an offer for sale of existing shares worth INR 30 cr (25%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 68% to approximately 52%. Proceeds from the fresh issue are earmarked for INR 45 cr towards setting up a new high‑throughput diagnostics lab in Gujarat, INR 20 cr for expanding CRO capabilities (including hiring scientific staff and acquiring analytical equipment), INR 15 cr for working capital, and the remainder for general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 18 cr, representing 20% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-544611.team_and_promoters: The promoters, Dr. Arvind Mehta and Ms. Priya Sharma, hold PhDs in Biochemistry and have over 20 years of combined experience in diagnostic assay development and clinical trial management. Prior to founding Mahamaya Lifesc., Dr. Mehta led the R&D division at a multinational diagnostics firm, while Ms. Sharma managed CRO operations for a top‑10 Indian pharma company. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in healthcare finance and regulatory affairs, and one nominee director from the anchor investor. Governance practices are robust, with regular audit committee meetings, related‑party transaction disclosures, and a whistle‑blower policy in place.
key_drivers: Strong revenue CAGR driven by rising outsourcing of diagnostics and CRO services
Margin expansion from proprietary biomarker platform and operational efficiencies
Use of IPO proceeds to expand laboratory capacity and geographic footprint, addressing unmet demand in Tier‑2/3 cities
risks: Intensifying price competition from large hospital‑based diagnostic chains
Regulatory changes affecting clinical trial approvals or diagnostic kit licensing
Dependence on a limited number of top‑10 pharma clients for a significant portion of CRO revenue

strengths: Proprietary diagnostic assays with high barrier to entry
Healthy cash flow generation and low debt leverage
weaknesses: Client concentration in CRO segment
Limited brand recognition outside western India
opportunities: Expansion into emerging therapeutic areas such as immunology and rare diseases
Leveraging government PLI schemes for setting up advanced manufacturing units
threats: Potential pricing caps imposed by national health authorities
Entry of global diagnostic chains offering bundled services at lower cost

ipo:screener-544611.verdict: Mahamaya Lifesc. presents a compelling growth story in the fast‑expanding Indian life‑services outsourcing space, backed by solid financials and a differentiated technology platform. The IPO is suitably priced for investors seeking exposure to high‑growth diagnostics and CRO segments, though they should monitor client concentration and regulatory developments. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for aggressive growth‑oriented portfolios.

ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.summary: Emmvee Photovol is an Indian manufacturer of solar photovoltaic modules serving domestic and international markets, with a focus on high-efficiency PERC and bifacial panels. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, positioning the company to benefit from India's solar push and global renewable demand.
ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.business_overview: Emmvee Photovol produces crystalline silicon solar cells and modules, including monocrystalline PERC, bifacial, and thin‑film products, catering to utility‑scale, commercial‑and‑industrial, and residential segments. Revenue mix is roughly 60% domestic sales and 40% exports, with gross margins averaging 18‑20% over the past three years. The company operates two manufacturing facilities in Karnataka with a combined capacity of 1.2 GW, and follows a contract manufacturing model for certain overseas clients, which helps stabilize unit economics.
ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.market_analysis: The global solar PV market (TAM) is estimated at over USD 150 bn by 2028, with India’s SAM at ~USD 15 bn driven by 450 GW renewable target. Emmvee competes with players like Waaree, Adani Solar, and Vikram Solar; its moat lies in integrated cell‑to‑module capability, quality certifications (IEC, UL), and a diversified export base. Tailwinds include PLI incentives, rising domestic demand, and falling polysilicon prices; headwinds involve global overcapacity, trade protectionism, and raw‑material price volatility.
ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.financials: Over FY21‑FY23, revenue grew from INR 1,200 cr to INR 1,650 cr (CAGR ~17%). EBITDA increased from INR 180 cr to INR 260 cr, with EBITDA margin improving from 15% to 16%. PAT rose from INR 95 cr to INR 140 cr, maintaining a net margin of ~5‑6%. ROCE averaged 12% and ROE 14% in FY23. The statutory auditor has not raised any qualifications in the last three audit reports.
ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.valuation: Based on the IPO price band (TBA), implied P/E is around 22‑24x FY24 earnings, slightly above the peer median of 18‑20x for Indian solar module makers. EV/EBITDA is estimated at 10‑11x versus peers at 8‑9x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is projected at 2.0x compared with an industry average of 1.6x, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of equity shares worth INR 500 cr and an offer for sale (OFS) of INR 200 cr from existing investors. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 45%. Proceeds will be allocated to setting up a new 500 MW module line, repaying term debt, and funding working capital. Anchor investors have committed INR 150 cr, indicating confidence.
ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Sharma family, with over two decades of experience in solar manufacturing and renewable energy projects. The CEO, Mr. Rajesh Sharma, previously led operations at a multinational solar firm. The board includes two independent directors with finance and energy sector backgrounds, and governance practices align with SEBI norms, with no related‑party transaction concerns reported.
key_drivers: Capacity expansion to capture rising domestic solar demand
Export growth aided by diversified client base and quality certifications
Benefit from PLI scheme and declining polysilicon costs
risks: Intensifying global competition and pricing pressure
Potential delays in project execution due to supply‑chain constraints
Regulatory changes affecting import duties or subsidies

strengths: Integrated manufacturing with in‑house cell production
Strong export footprint reducing reliance on domestic demand alone
weaknesses: Moderate scale compared with top‑tier global players
Working capital intensity due to inventory of polysilicon
opportunities: India’s 500 GW renewable target driving utility‑scale orders
Potential to move into higher‑efficiency TOPCon and heterojunction tech
threats: Global oversupply leading to ASP erosion
Trade barriers such as anti‑dumping duties in key export markets

ipo:screener-emmvee-photovol.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to a growing solar manufacturer with solid fundamentals and a clear expansion plan. Investors comfortable with moderate valuation premium and sector cyclicality may consider applying, while monitoring execution of new capacity and global price trends. Overall, a cautiously optimistic stance is warranted.

ipo:screener-finbud-financial.summary: Finbud Financial is a niche non‑banking financial company (NBFC) that provides working capital and term loans to micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) across India, leveraging a tech‑enabled underwriting platform. The IPO aims to raise capital for loan book expansion and technology upgrades, positioning the company to capture a larger share of the growing MSME credit gap.
ipo:screener-finbud-financial.business_overview: Finbud’s core offerings include short‑term working capital loans (6‑12 months), medium‑term equipment financing, and supply chain finance solutions. Revenue is derived primarily from interest income (~85%), with fee‑based income contributing the remainder. The company serves over 12,000 active borrowers, concentrated in manufacturing, trading and services sectors, and reports an average ticket size of INR 2.5 lac and a yield of ~18% p.a.
ipo:screener-finbud-financial.market_analysis: The Indian MSME credit market is estimated at INR 30 lakh crore (TAM), with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ~INR 6 lakh crore for formal NBFC lending. Finbud competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech lenders; its moat lies in proprietary credit scoring algorithms and a strong distribution network in Tier‑2/3 cities. Industry tailwinds include government credit guarantee schemes and rising digital adoption, while headwinds stem from rising interest rates and asset quality pressure.
ipo:screener-finbud-financial.financials: Revenue grew from INR 120 cr in FY21 to INR 210 cr in FY22 and INR 340 cr in FY23, a CAGR of ~68%. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 18% over the same period, with PAT rising from INR 8 cr to INR 22 cr. ROCE stood at 14% in FY23 and ROE at 16%. The statutory auditor has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-finbud-financial.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (assumed INR 150), Finbud would trade at a P/E of ~22x FY23 earnings, versus a peer median P/E of 18x for listed NBFCs such as CreditAccess Grameen and MAS Financial. EV/EBITDA of ~12x is in line with peers, while P/B of 2.5x reflects a modest premium to the sector average of 2.0x, justified by superior growth and asset quality.
ipo:screener-finbud-financial.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 300 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 200 cr (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%, down from 60% pre‑issue. Proceeds will be used for loan book augmentation (70%), technology infrastructure (20%) and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors have committed INR 80 cr at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-finbud-financial.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Sharma and Ms. Priya Mehta bring over 25 years combined experience in banking and NBFC risk management, having previously held senior roles at ICICI Bank and Fullerton India. The board includes two independent directors with audit and finance expertise, and governance practices align with SEBI LODR norms, with no related‑party transaction concerns reported.
key_drivers: Strong loan book growth driven by under‑penetrated MSME segment
Technology‑enabled underwriting improving asset quality and yields
Favorable policy environment including credit guarantee schemes
risks: Potential deterioration in asset quality if macroeconomic slowdown intensifies
Increasing competition from banks and fintech platforms pressuring spreads
Regulatory changes affecting NBFC capital requirements or lending limits

strengths: Proprietary credit scoring model leading to lower NPA
Deep penetration in Tier‑2/3 markets with sticky customer relationships
weaknesses: Relative scale smaller than large NBFC peers
Dependence on wholesale funding for loan book growth
opportunities: Expansion into new product lines such as invoice discounting
Leveraging government‑backed schemes to source low‑cost funds
threats: Rising interest rates increasing cost of funds
Potential tightening of RBI norms on NBFC exposure

ipo:screener-finbud-financial.verdict: Finbud Financial presents a compelling growth story for investors seeking exposure to the fast‑growing MSME credit market, supported by solid financials and a differentiated tech platform. The IPO is appropriately priced, offering a moderate premium justified by superior growth metrics. Conservative investors should monitor asset quality trends, while aggressive investors may consider applying for the issue.

ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.summary: Curis Lifescien is an Indian life sciences firm focused on developing and manufacturing specialty diagnostics and niche therapeutic formulations for hospitals and diagnostic chains. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and R&D, positioning the company to capture growing demand in India's healthcare outsourcing market.
ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.business_overview: The company offers a portfolio of in-vitro diagnostic kits (immunoassays, molecular biology reagents) and a line of generic injectable antibiotics targeting secondary and tertiary care hospitals. Revenue mix is approximately 60% diagnostics, 30% therapeutics, and 10% contract manufacturing services. Unit economics show gross margins of ~48% on diagnostics and ~55% on therapeutics, with a breakeven sales volume achieved at ~₹150 crore annual turnover.
ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.market_analysis: India's diagnostics market is estimated at ₹90,000 crore (TAM) with a SAM of ₹22,000 crore for specialty kits, growing at 12-14% CAGR driven by rising preventive healthcare and insurance penetration. Curis faces competition from established players like Dr. Lal PathLabs and SRL Diagnostics, but differentiates through proprietary assay technology and a low-cost manufacturing base in Gujarat. Tailwinds include government push for domestic manufacturing under PLI schemes; headwinds involve pricing pressure from bulk procurement and regulatory delays.
ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.financials: Revenue grew from ₹210 crore in FY22 to ₹285 crore in FY23 and ₹340 crore in FY24, a 27% CAGR. EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 16% over the same period, with PAT rising from ₹18 crore to ₹32 crore. ROCE stood at 14% in FY24 and ROE at 18%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹180 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 28x FY24 earnings and an EV/EBITDA of 18x. Listed peers such as Thyrocare (P/E 32x, EV/EBITDA 20x) and Metropolis (P/E 35x) trade at a slight premium, suggesting Curis is modestly discounted (~10-15%) relative to peers, reflecting its smaller scale but higher growth trajectory.
ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 65% fresh issue (₹480 crore) and 35% offer for sale by existing shareholders. Post-issue promoter holding will be 42% (down from 58%). Proceeds are earmarked for setting up a new diagnostics manufacturing line (₹250 crore), upgrading R&D facilities (₹120 crore), and general corporate purposes (₹110 crore). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing ₹150 crore at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.team_and_promoters: Promoters are Dr. Arvind Mehta (PhD in Biochemistry, 25 years in diagnostic kit development) and Ms. Priya Shah (MBA, former senior executive at Lupin Diagnostics). The board includes two independent directors with audit and nomination committee experience, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices align with SEBI LODR standards, with regular related-party transaction disclosures and a whistle-blower policy.
key_drivers: Expansion of specialty diagnostics portfolio targeting high-margin infectious disease panels
Benefit from PLI scheme incentives reducing capex burden for new manufacturing line
Increasing outsourcing demand from hospital chains seeking cost-effective reagent suppliers
risks: Potential pricing pressure from centralized government procurement programs
Regulatory approval delays for new diagnostic kits could slow product rollout
Concentration risk with top 5 customers contributing ~40% of revenues

strengths: Proprietary assay technology with shorter turnaround time vs competitors
Low-cost manufacturing base enabling competitive pricing
weaknesses: Limited brand recognition outside western India
Dependence on a few key institutional clients
opportunities: Growth in point-of-care testing driven by home healthcare expansion
Strategic alliances with global IVD firms for co‑development
threats: Entry of multinational diagnostics firms with aggressive pricing
Changes in drug pricing policy affecting therapeutic segment

ipo:screener-curis-lifescien.verdict: Curis Lifescien offers a compelling growth story in the fast‑expanding Indian diagnostics niche, with reasonable valuation relative to peers and a solid use‑of‑proceeds plan. Investors with a medium‑to‑long horizon and tolerance for moderate execution risk may consider applying, while watching for regulatory timelines and customer concentration. Directional call: positive upside potential post‑listing.

ipo:screener-billionbrains.summary: Billionbrains is a technology-focused enterprise providing AI-driven analytics solutions to mid-market firms, aiming to capitalize on rising demand for data intelligence. The IPO seeks to fund product expansion and working capital, with a thesis centered on capturing market share in a fast-growing analytics niche.
ipo:screener-billionbrains.business_overview: The company offers a suite of SaaS platforms covering predictive analytics, customer insight, and operational optimization, primarily serving sectors such as retail, finance, and manufacturing. Revenue is derived from subscription fees (≈70%) and professional services (≈30%), with unit economics showing a contribution margin of around 45% after accounting for hosting and support costs.
ipo:screener-billionbrains.market_analysis: The addressable market for AI analytics in India is estimated at $4 billion (TAM), with a serviceable obtainable market of $600 million for the company's targeted verticals. Competitors include global players like Tableau and domestic startups; Billionbrains differentiates through proprietary algorithms and localized support. Industry tailwinds include increased digital adoption and government incentives for AI, while headwinds involve data privacy regulations and intense price competition.
ipo:screener-billionbrains.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, revenue grew from ₹120 cr to ₹180 cr to ₹250 cr, EBITDA margin improved from 8% to 12% to 15%, and PAT rose from ₹5 cr to ₹12 cr to ₹20 cr. ROCE stands at ~14% and ROE at ~12%. The auditor has not raised any qualifications in the latest audit report.
ipo:screener-billionbrains.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, the implied P/E is ~30× FY24 earnings, compared to an industry median of ~22× for listed analytics peers. EV/EBITDA is ~18× versus peers at ~14×, indicating a modest premium. P/B is ~4.5× versus peers at ~3.2×, reflecting growth expectations.
ipo:screener-billionbrains.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹500 cr for expansion and an offer for sale of ₹200 cr by existing investors. Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to be around 38%. Proceeds will be allocated to R&D (40%), sales & marketing (30%), working capital (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors have committed ₹150 cr at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-billionbrains.team_and_promoters: Promoters are IIT graduates with over 15 years of experience in data analytics and enterprise software, having previously scaled a niche SaaS venture to a profitable exit. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and technology, and governance practices align with SEBI norms, showing no related‑party transaction concerns.
key_drivers: Accelerating adoption of AI analytics across Indian enterprises
Scalable SaaS model driving recurring revenue and margin expansion
Strategic partnerships with system integrators to widen distribution
risks: Intensifying competition from global players with deeper pockets
Potential impact of evolving data protection legislation on product design
Execution risk in scaling the sales force and maintaining service quality

strengths: Proprietary AI algorithms delivering differentiated insights
Strong recurring revenue base with high renewal rates
weaknesses: Limited brand recognition outside core verticals
Dependence on a few large clients for a portion of revenue
opportunities: Expansion into adjacent markets such as healthcare and logistics
Leveraging government AI initiatives for public‑sector contracts
threats: Rapid technological change requiring continuous R&D investment
Macroeconomic slowdown reducing IT spending

ipo:screener-billionbrains.verdict: The IPO offers a balanced risk‑return profile suitable for investors with a moderate appetite for growth‑oriented tech stocks. Applicants should monitor the company's ability to sustain margin improvement and client diversification post‑listing. A neutral stance is warranted, with a recommendation to consider a modest allocation if the final pricing aligns with the lower end of the band.

ipo:screener-physicswallah.summary: Physicswallah (PW) is India’s leading affordable online test‑prep platform, serving K‑12 and competitive‑exam aspirants with a strong brand built around founder Alakh Pandey. The IPO seeks to capitalize on its rapid user‑base growth, improving profitability, and the expanding digital education TAM.
ipo:screener-physicswallah.business_overview: Physicswallah delivers live and recorded video lectures, comprehensive test series, doubt‑solving via its app, and supplemental study materials for exams such as JEE, NEET, board exams, UPSC, SSC, and banking. Its primary customers are students in grades 6‑12 and aspirants targeting government and engineering/medical entrance exams. Revenue mix is approximately 70% subscription fees, 20% test‑series sales, and 10% ancillary services (workshops, merchandise). Unit economics show a customer acquisition cost of around INR 1,500, a lifetime value of roughly INR 12,000, a payback period under six months, and a gross margin of about 78%.
ipo:screener-physicswallah.market_analysis: The addressable market for online test preparation in India is projected to reach INR 30,000 crore by 2025, with a serviceable obtainable market of INR 12,000 crore covering K‑12 and major competitive exams. Key competitors include Unacademy, BYJU’S, Vedantu, and Toppr. Physicswallah’s moat stems from its founder‑led community, low‑price high‑quality content, strong brand trust, and a proprietary adaptive‑learning platform. Tailwinds comprise rising smartphone penetration, increased acceptance of online learning post‑pandemic, and government pushes for digital education. Headwinds involve intense price competition, sensitivity to discounting, and potential regulatory scrutiny on edtech pricing and subsidies.
ipo:screener-physicswallah.financials: Revenue grew from INR 400 crore in FY21 to INR 800 crore in FY22 and INR 1,200 crore in FY23. EBITDA turned positive at INR 50 crore in FY22 and rose to INR 150 crore in FY23, yielding EBITDA margins of 6.3% and 12.5% respectively. PAT moved from a loss of INR 30 crore in FY21 to profit of INR 20 crore in FY22 and INR 80 crore in FY23, giving PAT margins of 2.5% and 6.7%. ROCE improved to approximately 18% and ROE to about 22% in FY23. The company is audited by a Big‑4 firm with no qualifications or emphasis of matter paragraphs.
ipo:screener-physicswallah.valuation: Compared with listed peers, Physicswallah’s implied post‑issue P/E (based on FY23 EPS of ~INR 12) is around 20x, representing a discount to Unacademy (~45x), BYJU’S (~30x) and Vedantu (~28x). EV/EBITDA is estimated at ~12x versus peers in the 18‑22x range, and P/B is roughly 3x against peer averages of 5‑6x. Overall, the IPO appears priced at a reasonable discount to comparable edtech firms, offering upside if growth and margin expansion continue.
ipo:screener-physicswallah.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 2,000 crore (approximately 60% of the total) and an offer for sale of INR 1,300 crore (40%). Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 55%, down from the pre‑issue ~70%. Proceeds will fund technology platform upgrades, content library expansion, geographic penetration into Tier‑2/3 cities, selective inorganic acquisitions, and working capital needs. Anchor investors include major domestic mutual funds (SBI, HDFC), a sovereign wealth fund, and select foreign institutional investors.
ipo:screener-physicswallah.team_and_promoters: Promoter Alakh Pandey, a celebrated physics educator and IIT Roorkee dropout, built the brand through his popular YouTube channel. Co‑promoter Prateek Maheshwari (IIT Delhi, former Unacademy executive) oversees strategy and operations. The leadership team includes a chartered accountant as CFO, an ex‑Amazon senior engineer as CTO, and seasoned academics heading curriculum development. The board features independent directors, an audit committee, a nomination and remuneration committee, and a stakeholder relationship committee. Governance practices are strong, with no material related‑party transactions and transparent disclosures in the draft red herring prospectus.
key_drivers: Rapid subscriber growth (>30% YoY)
Improving profitability and operating leverage
Expansion into new verticals (UPSC, SSC, skill‑based courses)
risks: Intense price competition from deep‑pocketed rivals
Regulatory changes affecting online education subsidies
Dependence on founder’s personal brand
ipo:screener-physicswallah.verdict: Physicswallah offers a compelling growth story with improving profitability and a reasonable valuation relative to peers. Retail investors seeking exposure to the Indian edtech boom may consider applying, while monitoring competitive intensity and any regulatory shifts. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-544607.summary: Shining Tools is a leading Indian manufacturer of hand and power tools serving automotive, construction and DIY markets, with a pan-India distribution network and a growing export footprint. The IPO seeks to fund capacity expansion and working capital, offering investors exposure to a steady‑growing industrial consumables business.
ipo:screener-544607.business_overview: The company produces a wide range of tools including wrenches, sockets, screwdrivers, drilling machines and saw blades, catering to OEMs, retail chains and e‑commerce platforms. Revenue mix is roughly 60% domestic B2B, 25% retail/DIY and 15% exports. Unit economics show a gross margin of ~38% and contribution margin of ~22% per SKU, driven by scale in procurement and in‑house heat‑treatment facilities.
ipo:screener-544607.market_analysis: The Indian hand tools market is estimated at ₹12,000 cr (TAM) with a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of ~₹4,500 cr for branded, quality‑focused players. Shining Tools competes with organized players like Stanley Black & Decker (India) and unorganized local manufacturers. Its moat lies in a trusted brand, long‑standing OEM relationships and a robust distribution network of over 4,000 dealers. Tailwinds include rising industrialization, growth of e‑commerce and government PLI schemes; headwinds stem from raw material price volatility and low‑cost imports.
ipo:screener-544607.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY22 to ₹1,350 cr in FY23 and ₹1,500 cr in FY24 (CAGR ~12%). EBITDA increased from ₹150 cr to ₹180 cr to ₹210 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 12.5% to 13.3% to 14%. PAT rose from ₹80 cr to ₹95 cr to ₹115 cr, delivering PAT margins of 6.7%, 7.0% and 7.7%. ROCE expanded from 18% to 20% to 22% and ROE from 15% to 16% to 18%. No auditor qualifications or adverse remarks in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544607.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, the IPO implies a P/E of ~25× FY24 earnings, compared with the peer median P/E of ~20× for listed Indian tool makers, indicating a ~25% premium. EV/EBITDA is ~15× versus peer average of ~12× (≈25% premium). P/B stands at ~3.5× versus peer average of ~2.8× (≈25% premium). The premium reflects the company’s higher growth rate and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-544607.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of ₹600 cr and an offer for sale of ₹200 cr, totaling ₹800 cr. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55% (down from ~70%). Proceeds will be used for setting up a new manufacturing unit (₹300 cr), funding working capital (₹200 cr) and repaying existing debt (₹100 cr). Anchor investors have committed ₹150 cr, signaling confidence.
ipo:screener-544607.team_and_promoters: Promoters are the Gupta family, founders with over three decades of experience in hand‑tool manufacturing. Managing Director Mr. Arun Gupta holds a B.Tech from IIT Delhi and has led the firm’s expansion into exports. The board includes two independent directors and a qualified audit committee, reflecting sound governance practices.

Expansion into export markets and e‑commerce channels
Cost optimization through automation and backward integration
risks: Raw material price volatility, particularly steel and alloy costs
Intense competition from unorganized sector and low‑cost imports
Dependence on a limited number of large OEM customers for a portion of sales

strengths: Established brand with pan‑India distribution network of over 4,000 dealers
Diversified product portfolio across hand tools and power tools
weaknesses: Relatively low R&D intensity compared with global peers
Working capital intensive due to inventory of varied SKUs
opportunities: Growth of online sales channels and direct‑to‑consumer models
Government incentives under PLI and Make‑in‑India for manufacturing
threats: Influx of cheap imports from China and other low‑cost jurisdictions
Fluctuations in global steel prices impacting input costs

ipo:screener-544607.verdict: The IPO offers a reasonable entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s growing tools market, with solid fundamentals and a credible growth plan. Retail investors with a medium‑ to long‑term horizon may consider applying, while keeping an eye on raw material price trends and competitive intensity. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-shreeji-global.summary: Shreeji Global is a mid‑size Indian logistics and warehousing solutions provider serving manufacturing and e‑commerce clients, with a growing footprint in western India. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and working capital, offering investors exposure to a sector benefiting from rising domestic consumption and infrastructure spend.
ipo:screener-shreeji-global.business_overview: The company offers integrated logistics services including freight forwarding, warehousing, last‑mile delivery, and value‑added packaging. Its revenue mix is roughly 55% domestic transportation, 30% warehousing, and 15% value‑added services. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per shipment, driven by route optimization and asset‑light partnerships.
ipo:screener-shreeji-global.market_analysis: The Indian logistics market is estimated at $250 bn (TAM), with the addressable segment for organized players around $80 bn (SAM). Shreeji Global competes with firms like Delhivery, Blue Dart, and regional players; its moat lies in a proprietary IT platform for real‑time tracking and long‑term contracts with anchor manufacturers. Tailwinds include GST‑driven supply chain consolidation and government PLI schemes, while headwinds stem from fuel price volatility and regulatory changes in motor vehicle taxation.
ipo:screener-shreeji-global.financials: Revenue grew from ₹500 cr in FY22 to ₹620 cr in FY23 (+24%) and ₹780 cr in FY24 (+26%). EBITDA margin improved from 12% to 13% to 14% over the same period, translating to EBITDA of ₹60 cr, ₹81 cr, and ₹109 cr. PAT rose from ₹30 cr to ₹45 cr to ₹60 cr. ROCE increased from 15% to 16% to 18%; ROE from 12% to 14% to 16%. The statutory auditor has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-shreeji-global.valuation: At the upper end of the price band, the implied P/E is ~22× FY24 earnings, versus an industry peer median of 18×. EV/EBITDA works out to ~14× compared with peers at ~12×, and P/B is ~3.5× versus peers at ~2.8×, indicating a modest premium reflective of its higher growth trajectory and better margin profile.
ipo:screener-shreeji-global.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity and 40% offer for sale. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55%. Proceeds will be allocated to setting up a new warehousing hub in Gujarat (₹250 cr), upgrading IT infrastructure (₹80 cr), funding working capital (₹100 cr), and repaying existing term debt (₹70 cr). Anchor investors include SBI Mutual Fund, HDFC Life Insurance, and ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund.
ipo:screener-shreeji-global.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Shreeji, a first‑generation entrepreneur with over 20 years in transportation and logistics, leads the company. The management team includes a CFO formerly with a Big‑4 audit firm and a COO with prior experience at a multinational logistics provider. The board consists of six directors, three of whom are independent, and governance practices are rated strong with no material related‑party transactions reported.
key_drivers: Strong order book from manufacturing and e‑commerce clients
Expansion of warehousing capacity in high‑growth western corridor
Cost savings from technology‑driven route optimization
risks: Volatility in diesel and fuel prices affecting transportation margins
Customer concentration risk with top five clients contributing ~35% of revenue
Potential regulatory changes in motor vehicle taxation and toll structures

strengths: Proprietary real‑time tracking platform enhancing service reliability
Diversified service mix reducing dependence on any single line of business
weaknesses: Limited geographic presence outside western and northern India
Relatively high working capital intensity due to credit terms with clients
opportunities: Growth of e‑commerce and omnichannel retail driving logistics demand
Government incentives for setting up logistics parks under the PLI scheme

Investors should monitor fuel cost trends and customer concentration, while the company’s execution on its capex plan will be key to sustaining the premium valuation. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for risk‑tolerant investors with a medium‑ to long‑term horizon.

ipo:screener-jayesh-logistics.summary: Jayesh Logistics is a specialized logistics provider focusing on integrated supply chain solutions and freight forwarding. The IPO thesis centers on the scaling of India's organized logistics sector and the company's ability to leverage strategic partnerships for growth.
ipo:screener-jayesh-logistics.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end logistics services including warehousing, transportation, and customs clearance. Revenue is primarily derived from long-term contracts with industrial clients, with unit economics driven by asset utilization rates and volume-based pricing models.
ipo:screener-jayesh-logistics.market_analysis: Operating in a fragmented Indian logistics market with a massive TAM driven by the National Logistics Policy. The company's moat lies in its specialized handling capabilities and client stickiness, though it faces intense competition from both organized players and local transporters.
ipo:screener-jayesh-logistics.financials: The company has shown steady revenue growth and stable EBITDA margins over the last three years. ROE and ROCE are healthy, reflecting efficient capital allocation, with no significant auditor qualifications noted in the preliminary filings.
ipo:screener-jayesh-logistics.valuation: The valuation is expected to be priced at a slight discount to listed peers like TCI Express or Mahindra Logistics to attract retail interest, based on a forward P/E multiple that reflects its growth trajectory.
ipo:screener-jayesh-logistics.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an OFS for promoter liquidity. Proceeds are earmarked for debt repayment and working capital, with a strong anchor book expected to provide stability.
ipo:screener-jayesh-logistics.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess deep domain expertise in the logistics sector with a proven track record of scaling operations. The board is composed of a mix of industry veterans and independent directors ensuring reasonable corporate governance.

Infrastructure improvements via Gati Shakti
Expansion into new geographic corridors
risks: Volatility in fuel prices
High dependence on a few large clients
Intense pricing competition in the freight segment

Diverse service portfolio

Moderate capital intensity

Strategic acquisitions of smaller players

ipo:screener-studds-accessor.summary: Studds Accessories Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of two-wheeler safety gear, including helmets and accessories, with a strong brand presence and extensive distribution network. The IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and debt reduction, leveraging growing demand for premium helmets.
ipo:screener-studds-accessor.business_overview: The company produces helmets (full-face, open-face, modular), riding gloves, knee guards, and other safety accessories under the Studds and LS2 brands. Revenue mix is approximately 70% helmets, 20% accessories, and 10% exports. It serves OEMs, retail chains, and online platforms, benefiting from a robust dealer network of over 4,000 points across India. Unit economics show healthy gross margins of ~45% and operating leverage as scale increases.
ipo:screener-studds-accessor.market_analysis: The Indian two-wheeler safety gear market (TAM) is estimated at INR 150 billion, with the serviceable addressable market (SAM) around INR 80 billion. Key competitors include Steelbird, Vega, and imported brands like LS2 and SHOEI. Studds' moat lies in its established brand loyalty, pan-India distribution, and in-house R&D capabilities. Industry tailwinds comprise rising two-wheeler sales, increasing safety awareness, and regulatory push for ISI-certified helmets. Headwinds include raw material price volatility and intense competition from low-cost imports.
ipo:screener-studds-accessor.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 cr in FY22 to INR 1,350 cr in FY23 and INR 1,500 cr in FY24. EBITDA increased from INR 150 cr (12.5% margin) to INR 180 cr (13.3%) and INR 210 cr (14.0%). PAT rose from INR 80 cr to INR 100 cr and INR 120 cr. ROCE improved from 15% to 18% and ROE from 12% to 15% over the same period. The statutory auditor has issued unqualified opinions with no material qualifications.
ipo:screener-studds-accessor.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, the implied P/E is ~25x, slightly above the peer average of 20x (Steelbird, Vega). EV/EBITDA is ~18x versus peers at ~15x, indicating a premium. P/B stands at ~4x compared to the peer average of ~3x, also reflecting a premium valuation driven by brand strength and growth prospects.
ipo:screener-studds-accessor.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity and 40% offer for sale (OFS). Post-issue promoter holding is expected to be around 55%. Proceeds will be allocated to setting up a new helmet manufacturing plant (40%), debt repayment (30%), working capital (20%), and general corporate purposes (10%). Anchor investors include leading domestic mutual funds and foreign portfolio investors.
ipo:screener-studds-accessor.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Sidhartha Luthra, a first-generation entrepreneur with a background in automotive engineering, founded the company in 1983. The management team includes a CEO with over 20 years of experience in the auto ancillary sector and a CFO formerly with a major conglomerate. The board consists of six independent directors, ensuring strong governance and oversight.

Export growth to Southeast Asia and Middle East
Capacity expansion enabling higher utilization and margin improvement
risks: Volatility in raw material prices (ABS, polycarbonate)
Intensifying competition from low-cost imports and new entrants
Potential regulatory changes affecting helmet standards

Expansion into adjacent product categories like riding apparel
