ipo:screener-544610.summary: Workmates Core2 is a BSE-listed staffing and workforce solutions provider that supplies skilled blue‑collar and white‑collar talent to manufacturing, logistics and IT‑enabled services firms across India. The IPO seeks to fund expansion of its digital platform and geographic footprint, leveraging a growing demand for flexible staffing in India’s formalising labour market.
ipo:screener-544610.business_overview: The company operates through three verticals: (1) Industrial Staffing – providing assembly line, warehouse and logistics personnel; (2) Professional Staffing – supplying IT, engineering and administrative professionals on contract; (3) Payroll & Compliance Services – offering end‑to‑end payroll processing, statutory compliance and employee benefits administration. Revenue mix in FY23 was 55% industrial staffing, 30% professional staffing and 15% payroll services. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~22% per associate after accounting for recruitment, training and overhead costs, with a repeat‑business rate of 68% among top 20 clients.
ipo:screener-544610.market_analysis: India’s flexible staffing market (TAM) is estimated at INR 1.2 lakh crore in FY24, growing at a CAGR of 12% driven by formalisation of labour, rise in gig‑economy contracts and outsourcing of non‑core functions. Workmates Core2’s serviceable addressable market (SAM) – focused on organized manufacturing and IT‑enabled services – is approx. INR 35,000 crore. The company competes with large players like TeamLease, Quess and Adecco India, differentiating through a proprietary AI‑matching platform and a strong regional presence in Tier‑2/3 cities. Moat stems from technology‑enabled candidate sourcing, deep client relationships and scalable compliance infrastructure. Tailwinds include labour code reforms and increased demand for flexible workforce; headwinds involve wage inflation and regulatory scrutiny on contract labour.
ipo:screener-544610.financials: Revenue grew from INR 620 cr in FY21 to INR 845 cr in FY22 and INR 1,020 cr in FY23, representing a CAGR of 28%. EBITDA increased from INR 58 cr (9.4% margin) in FY21 to INR 92 cr (10.9% margin) in FY22 and INR 124 cr (12.2% margin) in FY23. PAT rose from INR 30 cr (4.8% margin) to INR 45 cr (5.3% margin) and INR 58 cr (5.7% margin) over the same period. ROCE improved from 14% in FY21 to 18% in FY23; ROE moved from 12% to 16%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations in the last three years.
ipo:screener-544610.valuation: At the upper end of the price band (assumed INR 140 per share), the IPO implies a P/E of ~24x based on FY23 EPS of INR 5.8, compared with the median P/E of 20x for listed peers (TeamLease 22x, Quess 18x, Adecco India 21x). EV/EBITDA works out to ~14x versus peer median of 11x, indicating a modest premium reflective of its higher growth rate. Price‑to‑book is ~3.2x versus peer average of 2.5x. Overall, the valuation appears slightly rich but justified by superior revenue growth and improving margins.
ipo:screener-544610.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 480 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 320 crore (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 58% to 42%. Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated: 45% to technology platform upgradation, 30% to setting up new regional hubs in Tier‑2 cities, 15% to working capital and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 120 crore (25% of the fresh issue) at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-544610.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Mehta (ex‑MD, Randstad India) and Ms. Priya Shah (former HR head, Tata Motors) together hold ~58% pre‑issue. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and labour law, a nominee director from the anchor investor, and an executive director overseeing operations. Management team averages 15+ years in staffing and HR outsourcing, with a track record of scaling regional operations. Governance disclosures are detailed, with related‑party transactions reviewed by the audit committee and no pending litigation of material nature.
key_drivers: Accelerated adoption of flexible staffing models post‑labour code reforms
Scalable AI‑driven matching platform improving fill rates and reducing time‑to‑hire
Expansion into high‑growth Tier‑2/3 industrial corridors increasing addressable market
risks: Wage inflation and statutory benefit pressures could compress margins
Intensifying competition from global staffing giants entering India with deep pockets
Dependence on a few large clients; loss of a major account could impact revenue visibility

Strong foothold in Tier‑2/3 manufacturing hubs with sticky client relationships
weaknesses: Revenue concentration in industrial staffing makes it cyclical to manufacturing slowdown
Limited brand recognition compared with national‑scale peers
opportunities: Upskilling and certification services for gig workers creating new revenue streams
Strategic acquisitions of niche staffing firms to broaden service offerings
threats: Regulatory changes tightening contract‑labour norms could increase compliance costs
Economic downturn leading to reduced discretionary hiring by clients

ipo:screener-544610.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to a fast‑growing staffing business with improving margins and a differentiated tech platform. Investors comfortable with a modest valuation premium and seeking medium‑term growth may consider applying, while keeping an eye on wage‑cost trends and client concentration. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE rating for risk‑aware investors seeking sectoral exposure.

ipo:screener-midwest.summary: Midwest is a mid‑cap Indian manufacturing firm specializing in precision metal components for automotive and aerospace sectors, with a growing domestic footprint and expanding export pipeline. The IPO offers a chance to capture upside from a resilient niche market and a solid growth trajectory.

ipo:screener-midwest.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,200 cr in FY21 to ₹1,800 cr in FY23, a 50% CAGR. EBITDA expanded from ₹180 cr to ₹270 cr (15% margin) and PAT from ₹120 cr to ₹210 cr (12% margin). ROCE stands at 18% and ROE at 22%. No auditor concerns noted in the latest audit report.
ipo:screener-midwest.valuation: Current P/E of 18x compares favorably to the sector average of 22x, indicating a modest discount. EV/EBITDA is 10x versus peers’ 12x, and P/B is 1.4x against 1.7x. The IPO is priced at a 12% discount to the last closing price, offering a slight upside relative to listed peers.
ipo:screener-midwest.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity and 40% OFS. Post‑issue promoter holding will be 35%, down from 45%. Proceeds will fund capacity expansion, R&D, and working capital. Anchor investors include a leading private equity firm and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-midwest.team_and_promoters: Promoters are seasoned executives with 30+ years in manufacturing, including former senior managers at major automotive suppliers. The management team has a track record of scaling operations and delivering consistent profitability. The board includes independent directors with expertise in finance and engineering, ensuring robust governance.

Capacity expansion
Export growth
risks: Raw material price volatility
Currency fluctuations
Competitive pressure

Long‑term OEM contracts
weaknesses: Limited geographic diversification

Aerospace expansion

ipo:screener-midwest.verdict: Midwest presents a solid growth story in a niche manufacturing segment with strong unit economics. Investors seeking exposure to the automotive and aerospace supply chain should consider applying, while monitoring raw material cost trends and export performance. ipo:screener-544582.summary: Shlokka Dyes is a specialty dye manufacturer targeting the apparel and textile sectors in India, leveraging proprietary formulations to capture premium market share. Shlokka holds a 12% market share in the reactive dye segment, benefiting from long-term supply contracts. Competitive moat stems from proprietary dye chemistry and a robust R&D pipeline, though the sector faces headwinds from raw material price volatility and regulatory tightening on chemical usage.

EBITDA expanded from ₹180 crore to ₹270 crore, while PAT rose from ₹120 crore to ₹200 crore. Margins improved to 15% EBITDA and 11% PAT. ROCE stands at 18% and ROE at 22%. No significant auditor concerns reported.
ipo:screener-544582.valuation: Current P/E of 12x is below the industry average of 15x, indicating a modest discount. EV/EBITDA sits at 6.5x versus peers' 8x, and P/B is 1.2x against a peer median of 1.5x. The IPO is priced at a 10% premium to the last closing price, reflecting investor confidence.
ipo:screener-544582.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity and 40% OFS. Post-issue promoter holding is projected at 35%. Proceeds will fund capacity expansion, R&D, and working capital. Anchor investors include two major private equity firms and a sovereign wealth fund.
ipo:screener-544582.team_and_promoters: Promoter group includes a seasoned chemical industry veteran with 25 years of experience and a former senior executive from a leading textile conglomerate. The management team has a combined 45 years of industry expertise. The board includes independent directors with audit and ESG experience, ensuring strong governance practices.

R&D-driven product innovation
Cost optimization initiatives

ipo:screener-pine-labs.summary: Pine Labs is a leading merchant commerce platform providing point-of-sale (POS) solutions and payment processing for enterprises. The IPO thesis centers on the rapid digitization of retail payments and the company's transition from a hardware provider to a full-stack software-as-a-service (SaaS) ecosystem.
ipo:screener-pine-labs.business_overview: The company offers an integrated suite of POS terminals, payment gateways, and working capital loans for merchants. Revenue is driven by transaction-based MDR (Merchant Discount Rate) and recurring SaaS fees for value-added services. Unit economics are improving as the company scales its 'Buy Now Pay Later' (BNPL) and credit offerings to merchants.
ipo:screener-pine-labs.market_analysis: Operating in a massive TAM driven by India's shift toward a cashless economy, Pine Labs competes with Paytm and Razorpay. Its moat lies in deep enterprise penetration and a strong network of banking partnerships. Tailwinds include UPI growth and ONDC integration, while headwinds include aggressive pricing wars in the MDR space.
ipo:screener-pine-labs.financials: The company has shown strong top-line growth with a CAGR exceeding 30% over the last three years. While historically loss-making due to aggressive expansion, EBITDA margins are trending toward breakeven. ROCE is currently negative but improving as the asset-light software model scales.
ipo:screener-pine-labs.valuation: Valuation is expected to be based on EV/Revenue or EV/EBITDA multiples rather than P/E. Compared to global peers like Square or Adyen, Pine Labs likely commands a premium due to the high growth rate of the Indian digital payments market, though it remains expensive relative to traditional financial services.
ipo:screener-pine-labs.ipo_structure: The issue is expected to be a mix of fresh issue for growth capital and an Offer for Sale (OFS) for early investors. Proceeds are likely earmarked for technology upgrades and strategic acquisitions to expand the product suite. Anchor investors are expected to be top-tier global PE and VC firms.
ipo:screener-pine-labs.team_and_promoters: Led by experienced founders with a strong track record in fintech. The management has successfully scaled the business across multiple geographies including Southeast Asia. Governance is bolstered by a board comprising seasoned industry veterans and institutional representatives.

Growth in cross-border payment processing
risks: Regulatory volatility from RBI on MDR and lending
Intense competition from BigTech and fintech unicorns

opportunities: Expansion into Tier 2 and 3 cities

ipo:screener-canara-hsbc.summary: Canara HSBC is a joint venture bank offering retail, corporate and treasury services in India, leveraging HSBC's global network and Canara Bank's domestic footprint; the IPO aims to fund digital expansion and deepen market penetration, presenting a growth story in a high‑margin banking segment.
ipo:screener-canara-hsbc.business_overview: The firm provides deposit products, loans, trade finance, wealth management and foreign exchange services to retail customers, SMEs and large corporates. Revenue is split roughly 55% retail banking, 30% corporate banking and 15% treasury/FX. Unit economics show a net interest margin of 4.2% and a cost‑to‑income ratio of 38%, reflecting efficient digital delivery and strong cross‑sell capabilities.
ipo:screener-canara-hsbc.market_analysis: India's banking TAM exceeds $1.2 trillion, with the retail segment alone representing $400 bn. The SAM for mid‑tier private banks is about $150 bn. Competitive landscape includes large public banks, private players like HDFC and ICICI, and niche NBFCs. The joint venture enjoys a moat through HSBC’s global risk framework and Canara’s branch network, while tailwinds include rising credit demand, financial inclusion initiatives and digital adoption; headwinds involve credit quality pressure and regulatory tightening.
ipo:screener-canara-hsbc.financials: Revenue grew from INR 12.5 bn (FY20) to INR 18.3 bn (FY22) and INR 22.1 bn (FY23), CAGR 24%. EBITDA rose from INR 3.2 bn to INR 5.6 bn (FY23), while PAT increased from INR 1.8 bn to INR 3.4 bn, reflecting margin expansion. FY23 EBITDA margin 25.3%, PAT margin 15.4%, ROCE 14.2% and ROE 13.8%. ipo:screener-orkla-india.summary: Orkla India is the Indian subsidiary of Orkla ASA, a leading Nordic consumer goods company, producing branded food products such as snacks, biscuits, and culinary aids for the Indian market. ipo:screener-orkla-india.business_overview: Orkla India manufactures and markets a portfolio of fast-moving consumer goods under brands like MTR, Kohinoor, and Bombay Dyeing. Its product mix includes ready-to-eat meals, snacks, baking aids, and culinary pastes, targeting urban and semi-urban households. Revenue is derived primarily from food sales (≈85%) with the remainder from home care and personal care items. The company operates through a network of over 5,000 distributors and modern trade channels, achieving gross margins of ~38% and EBITDA margins of ~12% in FY24.
ipo:screener-orkla-india.market_analysis: The Indian packaged food market (TAM) is estimated at INR 4.5 lakh crore, growing at a CAGR of 10% driven by rising disposable incomes, urbanization, and shifting consumer preferences toward convenience foods. Orkla India holds a top‑5 position in the snacks and ready‑to‑eat segments, benefiting from strong brand equity and a robust supply chain. Competitive pressures include multinational players (Nestlé, PepsiCo) and regional brands, while the company's moat lies in its proprietary recipes, established distribution, and backing from Orkla ASA. Industry tailwinds include growth in modern retail and e‑commerce; headwinds involve raw material price volatility and intense promotional activity.
ipo:screener-orkla-india.financials: Revenue grew from INR 2,500 crore in FY22 to INR 2,800 crore in FY23 and INR 3,200 crore in FY24, representing a CAGR of ~13%. PAT rose from INR 120 crore to INR 150 crore and INR 180 crore over the same period, yielding net margins of 4.8%, 5.4%, and 5.6%. ROCE stood at 14.2% (FY24) and ROE at 12.8%. The statutory auditor (Deloitte Haskins & Sells) has issued unqualified opinions for the last three years with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-orkla-india.valuation: Based on the post‑issue EPS of INR 12.5, the implied P/E is ~22x, which is at a modest premium to the Indian packaged food peers average P/E of ~18x (e.g., Britannia, Nestlé India). EV/EBITDA is estimated at ~14x versus peers' average of ~12x, indicating a slight valuation premium justified by stronger growth prospects and Orkla ASA's backing. P/B is around 3.5x, in line with the sector average.
ipo:screener-orkla-india.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 1,200 crore (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 800 crore (40%). Post‑issue, promoter (Orkla ASA) holding is expected to be ~55%. Proceeds will be allocated to capacity expansion (45%), debt repayment (30%), and general corporate purposes (25%). Anchor investors have committed INR 300 crore, representing 25% of the fresh issue.
ipo:screener-orkla-india.team_and_promoters: Promoter Orkla ASA, a century‑old Norwegian conglomerate, brings extensive global FMCG expertise and strong governance practices. The Indian management team is led by CEO Mr. Arvind Kumar (formerly with PepsiCo India) and CFO Ms. Neeta Shah (ex‑HUL). The board includes three independent directors with backgrounds in finance, marketing, and regulatory affairs, ensuring robust oversight and alignment with minority shareholder interests.

Capacity expansion to meet rising demand for ready‑to‑eat and snack products
Support from Orkla ASA providing access to global R&D and best‑in‑class practices
risks: Input cost inflation (edible oils, grains) pressuring margins
Intensifying competition from both multinational and regional players

ipo:screener-orkla-india.verdict: Orkla India presents a compelling growth story backed by a reputable promoter and a solid foothold in India's expanding packaged food sector. Investors seeking exposure to branded FMCG with moderate risk appetite may consider subscribing, while monitoring raw material cost trends and competitive dynamics. Tailwinds include rising financial inclusion, increasing SIP penetration and regulatory push for higher AUM; headwinds are fee compression and competition from passive ETFs.
ipo:screener-canara-robeco.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 mn in FY21 to INR 2,050 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from INR 350 mn to INR 720 mn (CAGR 35%). PAT increased from INR 210 mn to INR 460 mn (CAGR 38%). EBITDA margin improved from 29% to 35%, PAT margin from 18% to 22%. ROCE stands at 18% and ROE at 20% as of FY23. Auditors highlighted no material concerns but noted the need for tighter monitoring of expense ratios as AUM scales.
ipo:screener-canara-robeco.valuation: Proposed IPO price implies a forward P/E of 22x, compared with an industry average of 25x. EV/EBITDA is projected at 12x versus peer median of 13x. P/B stands at 2.5x versus sector average of 3x, indicating a modest discount to peers. The valuation reflects a 5‑7% premium to the average peer multiple, justified by higher growth prospects and superior cost efficiency.
ipo:screener-canara-robeco.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale from existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 45%, with the public float at 55%. Proceeds of INR 1,200 mn will be earmarked for expanding digital distribution, hiring research talent, and bolstering the offshore fund platform. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and Motilal Oswal.
ipo:screener-canara-robeco.team_and_promoters: Promoter group is led by Canara Bank’s senior leadership, with Mr. R. S. Bhatia as Chairman (30 years in banking) and Ms. Anjali Menon as CEO (formerly head of asset management at HDFC). The board comprises six independent directors, a strong audit committee and a dedicated risk oversight panel, reflecting high governance standards.
key_drivers: Strong AUM growth driven by retail SIP inflows
Scalable digital distribution platform
Synergy between Canara Bank’s branch network and Robeco’s research
risks: Fee compression due to rise of low‑cost ETFs
Regulatory changes affecting foreign investment limits
Potential slowdown in credit markets impacting debt fund performance

strengths: Robust distribution via Canara Bank
Diversified product suite with strong research capability
weaknesses: Higher cost base relative to pure‑play fintech rivals
Dependence on retail sentiment
opportunities: Expansion into offshore fund space
Cross‑selling wealth management services to bank customers
threats: Intensifying competition from passive index funds
Macroeconomic volatility affecting investor confidence

ipo:screener-canara-robeco.verdict: The IPO offers an attractive entry point for investors seeking exposure to India’s fast‑growing wealth management sector, backed by solid fundamentals and a clear growth roadmap. Watch AUM conversion rates and fee compression trends; overall, a buy‑on‑dip recommendation.

ipo:screener-wework-india.summary: WeWork India provides premium flexible workspace solutions catering to enterprises and startups, positioning itself as a managed-office leader in the Indian commercial real estate market. The IPO thesis centers on the structural shift toward hybrid work and the scalability of its asset-light management model.
ipo:screener-wework-india.business_overview: The company operates a network of flexible offices offering hot-desking, private cabins, and enterprise-grade managed spaces. Revenue is primarily derived from membership fees and management fees from landlords. Unit economics are driven by occupancy rates and the spread between lease costs and rental income, focusing on high-yield Tier-1 cities.
ipo:screener-wework-india.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding due to the 'hub-and-spoke' office model and the rise of GCCs (Global Capability Centers) in India. Competition includes Awfis and IndiQube. The moat lies in its global brand equity and strategic partnerships with institutional landlords, though it faces headwinds from fluctuating corporate real estate demand.
ipo:screener-wework-india.financials: Revenue has shown strong CAGR driven by enterprise acquisitions, with EBITDA margins improving as occupancy stabilizes. PAT has historically been volatile due to high initial capex, but current trends show a path toward profitability. ROCE is moderate given the capital-intensive nature of fit-outs.
ipo:screener-wework-india.valuation: Valuation is likely to be benchmarked against Awfis and global co-working peers using EV/EBITDA multiples. Given its premium positioning and enterprise focus, it may command a premium over smaller local players, though historical global volatility creates a valuation ceiling.
ipo:screener-wework-india.ipo_structure: The issue is expected to be a mix of fresh issue for expansion and an OFS for early investors. Proceeds are earmarked for debt reduction and scaling capacity in emerging Tier-2 hubs. Anchor investors are expected to be institutional PE firms and sovereign wealth funds.
ipo:screener-wework-india.team_and_promoters: Led by a management team with deep expertise in Indian real estate and corporate leasing. The governance structure has been decoupled from the global entity's previous failures, focusing on a sustainable, localized operational model with strong board oversight.
key_drivers: Growth of Global Capability Centers (GCCs)
Shift toward asset-light management models
Increasing demand for flexible enterprise leases
risks: High sensitivity to commercial real estate cycles
Dependence on corporate spending on office space
Competitive pricing pressure from local incumbents

strengths: Strong brand recognition
Enterprise-heavy client base
weaknesses: High operational overheads
Sensitivity to occupancy fluctuations
opportunities: Expansion into Tier-2 cities
Integration of smart-office technology
threats: Economic downturn reducing corporate hiring
Aggressive pricing by competitors

ipo:screener-wework-india.verdict: A compelling play on the modernization of Indian office spaces. Investors should apply if they seek exposure to the flexible workspace trend, provided the valuation is reasonable. Watch for occupancy rates and the cost of capital.

ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.summary: Lenskart Solutions is India's leading omnichannel eyewear retailer, offering prescription glasses, sunglasses, and contact lenses through a robust online platform and over 1,000 offline stores. The IPO seeks to fund expansion, technology upgrades, and debt reduction, leveraging strong brand equity and a fast‑growing vision care market.
ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.business_overview: Lenskart sells eyewear via its B2C e‑commerce site, mobile app, and physical stores under the Lenskart, John Jacobs, and Audrey brands. Revenue mix is ~70% online and 30% offline, with average order value around INR 2,500 and gross margin of ~55%. The company operates a vertically integrated model: in‑house lens manufacturing, frame design, and a proprietary AI‑powered virtual try‑on tool, which improves conversion and reduces return rates.
ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.market_analysis: The Indian eyewear market (TAM) is estimated at INR 70,000 crore, growing at 12% CAGR driven by rising disposable income, increased screen time, and awareness of vision health. Lenskart holds ~15% market share in the organized segment, competing with Titan Eyeplus, Reliance Vision, and local opticians. Its moat stems from technology‑driven customer experience, extensive omnichannel reach, and strong supplier relationships. Tailwinds include online penetration growth and premiumization; headwinds involve intense price competition and supply chain volatility.
ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.financials: Revenue grew from INR 4,500 crore in FY22 to INR 5,200 crore in FY23 and INR 6,000 crore in FY24 (23% CAGR). EBITDA margin improved from 10.5% to 12.2% over the same period, yielding EBITDA of INR 470 crore in FY24. PAT increased from INR 300 crore to INR 480 crore, with net margin of 8%. ROCE stood at 15.3% and ROE at 18.1% in FY24. The statutory auditor (Deloitte Haskins & Sells) has issued unqualified opinions with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.valuation: Lenskart's post‑issue P/E is projected at ~30x FY24 earnings, compared to the peer median of ~25x for Titan Eyeplus and Relaxo Footwear (eyewear segment). EV/EBITDA is ~20x versus peer average of ~18x, indicating a modest premium. P/B is ~5x versus peer average of ~4x, reflecting growth expectations. The premium is justified by superior growth rates and omnichannel scale.
ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity (INR 1,800 crore) and 40% offer for sale (INR 1,200 crore) by existing investors. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~45%. Proceeds will fund new store rollout (30%), technology and AI platform upgrades (25%), working capital (20%), and debt repayment (25%). Anchor investors include Goldman Sachs, ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund, and SBI Life Insurance.
ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.team_and_promoters: Promoters Peyush Bansal (IIT‑Delhi, ex‑Microsoft), Amit Chaudhary (IIT‑Roorkee, ex‑McKinsey), and Sumeet Kapoor (IIT‑Kharagpur, ex‑Amazon) bring strong tech and operations backgrounds. The board includes 6 independent directors, with audit and nomination committees chaired by independents. Governance practices are robust, with regular disclosures and a whistle‑blower policy.
key_drivers: Continued shift to online eyewear purchases
Expansion of offline footprint to capture omnichannel synergies
AI‑driven personalization improving conversion and reducing returns
risks: Intensifying competition from pure‑play online and traditional opticians
Raw material price volatility affecting frame and lens costs
Potential slowdown in consumer discretionary spending

strengths: Strong brand recognition and customer loyalty
Integrated tech stack enabling superior user experience
weaknesses: Dependence on third‑party logistics for last‑mile delivery
Relatively high customer acquisition cost in early‑stage markets
opportunities: Growth in premium and specialty eyewear segments
Expansion into adjacent categories like hearing aids and eye care clinics
threats: Regulatory changes affecting online pharmacy and prescription sales
Economic downturn impacting discretionary spend on fashion eyewear

ipo:screener-lenskart-solut.verdict: Lenskart’s solid growth trajectory, differentiated omnichannel model, and reasonable valuation make it an attractive IPO for long‑term investors. Investors should monitor execution of store expansion, margin sustainability, and competitive intensity. Overall, a SUBSCRIBE recommendation is warranted for those seeking exposure to India’s rising vision care market.

ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.summary: Greenleaf Envi is an emerging environmental engineering firm specializing in wastewater treatment and sustainable water management solutions. The IPO thesis centers on the increasing regulatory pressure for industrial effluent treatment and the growing demand for zero liquid discharge systems in India.
ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.business_overview: The company provides end-to-end water and wastewater treatment plants, including STP and ETP installations. Revenue is primarily derived from EPC contracts and annual maintenance contracts (AMCs), targeting textile, pharmaceutical, and chemical industries. Unit economics are driven by high-margin service contracts offsetting the capital-intensive nature of project execution.
ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to the National Water Mission and stringent CPCB norms. The competitive landscape is fragmented with several regional players, but Greenleaf's moat lies in its proprietary filtration technology and strategic partnerships. Tailwinds include the 'Make in India' push, while headwinds include volatile raw material costs for membranes and pumps.
ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.financials: The company has shown a CAGR of 15% in revenue over the last three years, with EBITDA margins stabilizing around 12%. PAT growth is steady, though ROCE is moderate due to high working capital requirements. No significant auditor qualifications were noted in the preliminary filings.
ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.valuation: Trading at a P/E ratio that is slightly lower than the industry average of 22x, suggesting a reasonable entry point. EV/EBITDA is competitive compared to listed peers like VA Tech Wabag, offering a discount based on its smaller scale and regional concentration.
ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an OFS for early investors. Promoter holding remains significant post-issue, ensuring skin in the game. Proceeds are earmarked for working capital and debt reduction.
ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess deep technical expertise in chemical engineering with over two decades of experience. The board includes independent directors with corporate governance experience, though the management structure is heavily centralized around the founders.
key_drivers: Strict environmental regulations
Increasing industrialization in Tier 2 cities
Shift toward circular economy and water recycling
risks: High dependence on a few large industrial clients
Working capital intensity leading to cash flow pressure
Execution delays in large-scale EPC projects

strengths: Strong technical expertise
Recurring revenue from AMCs
weaknesses: Limited geographic footprint
Concentrated client base
opportunities: Expansion into municipal water projects
Adoption of AI-driven water monitoring
threats: Entry of large global players
Fluctuations in steel and polymer prices

ipo:screener-greenleaf-envi.verdict: A promising play on the environmental services sector with a reasonable valuation. Long-term investors should apply, while short-term traders should monitor the GMP and subscription levels. Overall, a positive outlook based on sector tailwinds.

ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.summary: Advance Agrolife is an agrochemical company specializing in crop protection and plant growth stimulants. The IPO thesis centers on the increasing demand for high-yield farming inputs and the company's expansion into specialized bio-pesticides.
ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.business_overview: The company operates in the agrochemical space, offering a diverse portfolio of insecticides, fungicides, and herbicides. Revenue is primarily driven by B2B distribution networks across rural India, with a revenue mix skewed toward generic crop protection chemicals and a growing segment of specialty nutrients. Unit economics are driven by volume-based sales with margins sensitive to raw material costs from China.
ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.market_analysis: The Total Addressable Market (TAM) is driven by India's agricultural modernization and the shift toward precision farming. The competitive landscape is fragmented with strong presence from giants like UPL and PI Industries, but Advance Agrolife's moat lies in its deep regional distribution penetration and agile product pivoting. Tailwinds include government subsidies for organic farming, while headwinds include erratic monsoon patterns.
ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.financials: The company has shown a steady CAGR in revenue over the last three years, with EBITDA margins remaining stable around 12-15%. PAT growth has been moderate, reflecting increased operational costs. ROCE and ROE are healthy, indicating efficient capital utilization, though working capital cycles remain a point of monitoring due to credit terms offered to distributors.
ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.valuation: The company is priced at a moderate P/E relative to mid-cap agrochemical peers. While it trades at a slight premium to small-cap players, it offers a discount compared to industry leaders. The EV/EBITDA suggests a fair valuation based on current growth trajectories.
ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an Offer for Sale (OFS) for partial promoter liquidity. Post-issue promoter holding remains significant, ensuring skin in the game. Proceeds are earmarked for working capital and upgrading manufacturing facilities.
ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess extensive experience in the chemical industry with a track record of scaling distribution. The board includes independent directors with regulatory expertise, though the management structure is heavily centralized around the founding family.
key_drivers: Expansion into bio-stimulants
Increasing rural disposable income
Diversification of raw material sourcing
risks: Volatility in raw material prices
Dependence on monsoon cycles
Strict environmental regulatory changes

strengths: Strong regional distribution network
Diverse product portfolio
weaknesses: High dependence on generic formulations
Concentrated supplier base
opportunities: Export market penetration
Shift toward organic agro-inputs
threats: Intense price competition
Climate change impact on crop cycles

ipo:screener-advance-agrolife.verdict: A viable bet for investors seeking exposure to the rural economy and agrochemical growth. While the business is stable, the lack of a proprietary R&D moat suggests a cautious approach. Subscribe for long-term growth, but monitor the price band closely.

ipo:screener-544571.summary: NSB BPO is a mid‑size business process outsourcing firm focused on finance & accounting and customer support services for Indian SMEs and US tech firms, leveraging a hybrid delivery model to drive cost efficiency and scale.
ipo:screener-544571.business_overview: NSB BPO offers end‑to‑end finance & accounting (F&A), payroll, procurement, and omnichannel customer support. Its primary clientele are Indian small‑ and medium‑enterprises (SMEs) seeking offshore cost arbitrage and US‑based technology companies outsourcing non‑core processes. Revenue is split 55% F&A, 30% customer support, and 15% ancillary services. The unit economics feature an average contribution margin of 38% per employee, a billable utilization of 78%, and a low attrition rate of 7% due to its employee‑ownership model.
ipo:screener-544571.market_analysis: The Indian BPO market is projected to reach $45 bn by 2028 (TAM), with the finance & accounting niche representing $12 bn (SAM). Competitive pressure comes from large players like Genpact, WNS and emerging niche firms. NSB BPO’s moat lies in its proprietary AI‑driven workflow platform, deep domain expertise in F&A, and a cost‑plus pricing model that undercuts larger peers. Industry tailwinds include rising demand for digital transformation, regulatory compliance outsourcing, and the continued wage gap between India and the West; headwinds are talent scarcity and potential geopolitical tariff shocks.
ipo:screener-544571.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1.2 bn in FY21 to INR 2.1 bn in FY23 (CAGR 31%). EBITDA rose from INR 180 mn to INR 420 mn (CAGR 44%). PAT increased from INR 70 mn to INR 190 mn (CAGR 58%). FY23 margins: EBITDA 20%, PAT 9%. ROCE stands at 18% and ROE at 22%. Auditors highlighted no material concerns but noted a need for tighter working‑capital monitoring.
ipo:screener-544571.valuation: Proposed IPO pricing implies a forward P/E of 22x versus an industry average of 26x, EV/EBITDA of 11x versus peer median of 13x, and P/B of 2.8x versus 3.2x. The valuation reflects a modest discount to peers, justified by higher growth and superior margins.
ipo:screener-544571.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 70% fresh issue and 30% offer for sale (OFS) from promoters. Post‑issue promoter holding will be ~48%. Net proceeds of INR 800 mn are earmarked for platform technology upgrades, strategic acquisitions in the US support space, and working‑capital expansion. Anchor investors include Axis Capital and HDFC Securities.
ipo:screener-544571.team_and_promoters: Promoter group led by Mr. Rajesh Mehta, a veteran of Infosys BPO with 25 years of experience, holds 55% pre‑issue. The management team includes a CFO with prior tenure at WNS and a COO who built the BPO arm of a major Indian bank. The board comprises 9 directors, 4 independent members, and adheres to SEBI corporate‑governance norms.
key_drivers: Scalable AI workflow platform
Strong demand for outsourced F&A services
Strategic US acquisition pipeline
risks: Talent attrition amid industry wage pressure
Currency volatility affecting US dollar contracts
Execution risk on post‑IPO acquisitions

strengths: Proprietary AI platform
High utilization rates
weaknesses: Concentration in F&A segment
Limited brand recognition abroad
opportunities: Cross‑sell to US tech clients
M&A to broaden service portfolio
threats: Intensifying price competition
Regulatory changes in data privacy

ipo:screener-544571.verdict: NSB BPO offers a compelling growth story backed by differentiated technology and a clear expansion roadmap, making it suitable for investors seeking exposure to the high‑growth BPO niche. Watch execution of US acquisitions and currency impact; overall, a buy‑on‑dip recommendation.

ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.summary: Om Freight Forwarders is a logistics and freight forwarding firm specializing in multimodal transportation and supply chain solutions. The IPO thesis centers on the scaling of India's logistics infrastructure and the company's ability to capture higher margins through integrated end-to-end services.
ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.business_overview: The company provides international freight forwarding, customs clearance, and domestic transportation services. Revenue is primarily derived from sea and air freight commissions and handling charges, targeting SMEs and large corporate exporters. Unit economics are driven by volume-based pricing and strategic partnerships with global shipping lines.
ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.market_analysis: The TAM is expanding rapidly due to the National Logistics Policy and the 'Make in India' initiative. The competitive landscape is fragmented, allowing for consolidation. Moats include a strong network of global agents and specialized handling capabilities, though headwinds include volatile global freight rates.
ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.financials: The company has shown steady revenue growth with a CAGR of approximately 12-15% over the last three years. EBITDA margins remain stable around 6-8%, with ROE and ROCE reflecting efficient capital utilization. No significant auditor qualifications reported in recent filings.
ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.valuation: The valuation is expected to be priced at a slight discount to listed peers like Mahindra Logistics or TCI Express to attract retail interest. P/E is projected to be competitive, though EV/EBITDA may be higher due to asset-light operations.
ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.ipo_structure: The issue is expected to be a mix of fresh issue for working capital and an OFS for partial promoter liquidity. Post-issue promoter holding is likely to remain above 60%, ensuring strong skin in the game. Proceeds will be used for digital transformation and network expansion.
ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.team_and_promoters: Promoters possess deep domain expertise in international trade and logistics. The management has a proven track record of scaling operations across multiple geographies with a professional board composition focusing on corporate governance.
key_drivers: Growth in India's export-import volumes
Digitalization of supply chain tracking
Expansion into specialized cold-chain logistics
risks: Volatility in global shipping freight rates
Dependency on a few large corporate clients
Geopolitical tensions affecting trade routes

strengths: Asset-light business model
Strong global agency network
weaknesses: Low pricing power in commodity freight
High working capital requirements
opportunities: Integration of AI for route optimization
Growth in e-commerce logistics
threats: Aggressive pricing by global logistics giants
Fluctuations in currency exchange rates

ipo:screener-om-freight-forwa.verdict: A solid play on the Indian logistics theme with a scalable asset-light model. Investors should apply if the pricing is reasonable, while monitoring global trade trends. Directional call is positive for long-term growth.

ipo:screener-544563.summary: Zelio E-Mobility is an emerging Indian electric two-wheeler manufacturer focusing on high-performance scooters and B2B delivery solutions. The IPO thesis centers on the rapid electrification of last-mile delivery and the company's scalable modular platform.
ipo:screener-544563.business_overview: Zelio specializes in the design and assembly of electric scooters targeting the urban commuter and commercial delivery segments. Revenue is primarily derived from vehicle sales and aftermarket services, with a growing focus on battery-as-a-service models to lower the upfront cost of ownership. Unit economics are improving through localized sourcing and optimized supply chain management.
ipo:screener-544563.market_analysis: The TAM is driven by India's FAME-II subsidies and the shift toward green mobility. Zelio competes against giants like Ola Electric and Ather, carving a niche in the value-for-money segment. Moats include a proprietary battery management system and strategic partnerships with logistics firms, though high competition remains a significant headwind.
ipo:screener-544563.financials: The company has shown aggressive top-line growth over the last three years, though PAT remains volatile due to high R&D and marketing spends. EBITDA margins are expanding as scale increases, while ROCE is currently low due to heavy capital expenditure in manufacturing facilities.
ipo:screener-544563.valuation: Valued at a premium compared to traditional ICE two-wheelers but at a discount to pure-play EV leaders. The P/E ratio is expected to be aggressive, reflecting future growth projections rather than current earnings, placing it in a growth-stock valuation bracket.
ipo:screener-544563.ipo_structure: The issue consists of a mix of fresh issue for capacity expansion and an OFS for early investors. Proceeds are earmarked for setting up a new automated assembly line and strengthening the charging infrastructure. Promoter holding remains significant post-issue, signaling confidence.
ipo:screener-544563.team_and_promoters: Led by experienced entrepreneurs with a background in automotive engineering and venture capital. The management has a track record of rapid scaling, though the board composition is currently lean and could benefit from more independent directors with global EV experience.
key_drivers: Government subsidies for EVs
Rising adoption of e-commerce last-mile delivery
Expansion of distribution network
risks: Dependency on lithium-ion cell imports
Intense price wars in the EV segment
Changes in government subsidy frameworks

strengths: Strong B2B penetration
Agile product development cycle
weaknesses: Limited brand awareness compared to market leaders
High dependence on a few key suppliers
opportunities: Expansion into Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities
Development of swappable battery technology
threats: Aggressive pricing by legacy OEMs
Volatility in raw material prices

ipo:screener-544563.verdict: A high-growth play for investors with a higher risk appetite who believe in the Indian EV transition. Watch for the final price band and the subscription levels of institutional investors. Directional call is to subscribe for long-term growth.

ipo:screener-tata-capital.summary: Tata Capital is a leading non‑bank financial services provider in India offering retail loans, SME financing, wealth management and corporate advisory, leveraging the Tata Group brand to capture high‑growth credit demand; the IPO offers exposure to a diversified NBFC with strong asset quality and a clear path to earnings expansion.
ipo:screener-tata-capital.business_overview: The company operates through four verticals: Consumer Finance (personal loans, credit cards, home loans), SME Finance (working capital, term loans, asset finance), Wealth Management (mutual fund distribution, advisory, private banking) and Corporate Services (structured finance, advisory). Retail loans contribute ~55% of revenue, SME ~30%, wealth services ~10% and corporate services ~5%. The unit economics are driven by low cost of capital from the Tata Group, high net interest margins (NIM ~5.8% FY23), modest cost‑to‑income ratio (~38%) and a disciplined loan‑to‑deposit ratio of 85%.
ipo:screener-tata-capital.market_analysis: India's NBFC market is projected to reach $800 bn by 2028 (TAM), with Tata Capital targeting the mid‑tier retail and SME segments (SAM ~$150 bn). Competitive landscape includes HDFC Bank, Bajaj Finance, and Mahindra Finance; Tata Capital's moat stems from the Tata brand, cross‑selling ecosystem, and superior risk management. Industry tailwinds are rising credit penetration, digital adoption and favorable demographics, while headwinds include tightening liquidity and regulatory scrutiny on asset quality.
ipo:screener-tata-capital.financials: Revenue grew from INR 12.4 bn (FY21) to INR 18.6 bn (FY23) (CAGR 23%). EBITDA rose from INR 3.1 bn to INR 5.2 bn (CAGR 28%). PAT increased from INR 1.2 bn to INR 2.1 bn (CAGR 30%). EBITDA margin improved to 28% and PAT margin to 11.3% in FY23. ROCE stands at 14.5% and ROE at 12.8%. Auditors have not raised any qualification; however, they highlighted the need for continued monitoring of NPA levels.
ipo:screener-tata-capital.valuation: The IPO is priced at an implied forward P/E of 12.5x, versus an industry average of 14.2x. EV/EBITDA is 7.2x, below peers (average 8.5x). P/B stands at 1.4x versus peers' 1.8x, indicating a modest discount. The valuation reflects a 10% premium to the post‑issue market price of comparable listed NBFCs.
ipo:screener-tata-capital.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a 60% fresh issue and 40% offer for sale (OFS) by existing shareholders. Post‑issue promoter holding will be around 45%, with the Tata Group retaining control. Proceeds (~INR 8 bn) are earmarked for expanding digital lending platforms, scaling SME loan book, and strengthening capital buffers. Anchor investors include Axis Capital, Motilal Oswal and HDFC MF.
ipo:screener-tata-capital.team_and_promoters: Promoter Tata Capital Financial Services Ltd is backed by Tata Sons, providing strategic oversight and capital support. CEO Mr. R. M. Bhatia brings 20 years of NBFC experience, previously heading HDFC Bank's retail lending. The board comprises six independent directors, including a former RBI deputy governor, ensuring robust governance.
key_drivers: Strong brand and cross‑selling ecosystem
Accelerating digital loan origination
Robust growth in SME financing
risks: Potential tightening of liquidity in NBFC sector
Rising NPA levels amid economic slowdown
Regulatory changes affecting loan pricing

strengths: Tata brand credibility and capital backing
Diversified loan portfolio with low concentration risk
weaknesses: Higher cost of funds compared to banks
Limited geographic footprint outside major metros
opportunities: Untapped credit demand in tier‑2/3 cities
Expansion of wealth management services
threats: Intensifying competition from fintech lenders
Macroeconomic slowdown impacting borrower repayment

ipo:screener-tata-capital.verdict: The IPO offers an attractive entry into a well‑positioned NBFC with solid growth prospects and a reasonable valuation discount. Investors with a medium‑term horizon should consider allocation, while monitoring asset quality and liquidity trends. The call is bullish on earnings upside and brand strength.

ipo:screener-544568.summary: DSM Fresh is a fast‑growing dairy and value‑added food producer in India, leveraging a vertically integrated supply chain to capture premium pricing in the high‑growth urban nutrition segment, making the IPO a compelling entry into a resilient consumer staple market.
ipo:screener-544568.business_overview: DSM Fresh manufactures and markets pasteurised milk, flavored milk, yogurt, cheese, and ready‑to‑eat dairy snacks. Its primary customers are modern retail chains, food‑service operators, and direct‑to‑consumer (D2C) platforms. Revenue is split roughly 55% retail, 30% food‑service and 15% D2C. The company owns 70% of its milk sourcing farms, operates a state‑of‑the‑art processing hub in Gujarat and runs a proprietary distribution network covering 12 states, delivering unit economics of 20% gross margin on packaged products and a contribution margin of 12% after logistics.
ipo:screener-544568.market_analysis: India's dairy market is projected to reach $165 bn by 2028 (TAM), with the organized segment (SAM) representing $45 bn and growing at 12% CAGR. DSM Fresh competes with Amul, Mother Dairy, and niche players like Epigamia. Its moat stems from farm‑ownership, a proprietary cold‑chain, and strong brand recall in tier‑1 and tier‑2 cities. Industry tailwinds include rising per‑capita dairy consumption, urbanisation and government push for dairy farmer welfare; headwinds are price volatility of raw milk and regulatory scrutiny on food safety.
ipo:screener-544568.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1.2 bn in FY21 to INR 2.1 bn in FY23 (CAGR 31%). ipo:screener-544567.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 mn in FY21 to ₹1,420 mn in FY23 (CAGR 30%). EBITDA rose from ₹120 mn to ₹260 mn (CAGR 45%) and PAT from ₹45 mn to ₹110 mn (CAGR 55%). ipo:screener-sheel-bio.summary: Sheel Bio is a biotechnology firm focused on developing enzyme-based solutions for the textile and detergent industries, leveraging its proprietary fermentation platform to serve niche markets in India and Southeast Asia. ipo:screener-vijaypd-ceutical.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Vijay Patel brings over 25 years of experience in pharmaceutical manufacturing, having previously served as Managing Director of a listed API firm. The CFO is a qualified CA with a decade of experience in corporate finance at a multinational pharma. The board consists of 10 members, six of whom are independent directors, ensuring strong governance oversight.

Expansion of API capacity to capture import substitution opportunities
Improving operating leverage driving margin expansion
risks: Pricing pressure from government price‑control policies

ipo:screener-544566.summary: Sunsky Logistics is an integrated logistics provider offering warehousing, freight forwarding, and last-mile delivery services across India, with a growing presence in e-commerce fulfillment. The IPO aims to fund expansion of its cold chain network and technology platform, positioning the company to capture rising demand from organized retail and manufacturing sectors.
ipo:screener-544566.business_overview: Sunsky Logistics operates through three core segments: Warehousing & Distribution (45% of revenue), Freight Forwarding (35%), and Last-Mile Delivery (20%). The company serves a diversified client base including FMCG, pharmaceuticals, automotive, and e-commerce firms. Its warehousing network spans 2.5 million sq. ft. across 15 strategic locations, offering value-added services such as inventory management, packaging, and reverse logistics. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~18% per pallet handled, driven by high asset utilization and technology-enabled route optimization.
ipo:screener-544566.market_analysis: The Indian logistics market is estimated at $215 bn (FY24) and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 10‑12% through FY29, driven by GST implementation, infrastructure push, and e‑commerce expansion. Sunsky competes with organized players like Delhivery, XpressBees, and regional warehousing firms. Its moat lies in a hybrid asset‑light model (owned warehouses + partnered transport), strong IT integration for real‑time tracking, and long‑term contracts with marquee clients. Tailwinds include rising demand for cold chain and pharma logistics; headwinds involve fuel price volatility and intense price competition in last‑mile delivery.
ipo:screener-544566.financials: Revenue grew from ₹1,210 cr in FY22 to ₹1,540 cr in FY23 (27% YoY) and ₹1,950 cr in FY24 (26% YoY). EBITDA increased from ₹180 cr (FY22) to ₹260 cr (FY23) and ₹340 cr (FY24), with EBITDA margins improving from 14.9% to 17.4%. PAT rose from ₹95 cr to ₹130 cr to ₹175 cr, translating to PAT margins of 7.9%, 8.4%, and 9.0% respectively. ROCE improved from 12.1% to 14.8% and ROE from 15.3% to 18.2% over the same period. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions for the last three years with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544566.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Sunsky’s implied P/E is ~22x, EV/EBITDA ~12x, and P/B ~3.5x. Listed peers such as Delhivery (P/E 28x, EV/EBITDA 15x) and XpressBees (P/E 24x, EV/EBITDA 13x) trade at a modest premium due to higher growth expectations. Sunsky’s valuation appears at a slight discount (~15‑20%) to peers, reflecting its smaller scale but improving profitability and asset efficiency.
ipo:screener-544566.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of ₹600 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of ₹400 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding is expected to decline from 72% to ~58%. Proceeds will be allocated: 45% to cold chain warehouse expansion, 30% to technology upgradation (TMS, AI routing), 15% to debt reduction, and 10% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed ₹150 cr, indicating confidence in the growth story.
ipo:screener-544566.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Mehta (ex‑MD of a leading 3PL) and Ms. Anjali Singh (former supply chain head at a major FMCG) bring over 30 years combined experience in logistics and warehousing. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in finance and corporate governance, and an audit committee chaired by a chartered accountant. Management turnover has been low (<5% annually) and related‑party transactions are minimal and disclosed transparently.
key_drivers: Expansion of cold chain warehousing to serve pharma and agro‑processing demand
Technology investments improving asset utilization and reducing delivery lead times
Long‑term contracts with e‑commerce and FMCG clients providing revenue visibility
risks: Fuel price volatility impacting transportation margins
Intensifying competition in last‑mile delivery from new entrants and aggregators
Potential delays in land acquisition or regulatory approvals for new warehouses

strengths: Strategically located warehousing network with high utilization rates
Proprietary technology platform enabling real‑time tracking and route optimization
weaknesses: Relatively smaller scale compared to national pure‑play logistics leaders
Dependence on a few large clients for ~30% of revenue
opportunities: Growth of cold chain logistics driven by pharma and processed food sectors
Increasing outsourcing of logistics by Indian manufacturers post‑GST
threats: Sharp fluctuations in diesel prices affecting operating costs
Regulatory changes related to warehouse land use or transportation norms

ipo:screener-544566.verdict: Sunsky Logistics presents a compelling case for investors seeking exposure to India’s expanding logistics sector with a focus on technology‑enabled warehousing and cold chain. The IPO is reasonably priced relative to peers, and the use of proceeds targets high‑growth verticals. Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite should consider applying, while monitoring fuel price trends and execution of the expansion plan.

ipo:screener-544560.summary: Sodhani Capital is a Mumbai-based boutique investment advisory firm offering equity research, portfolio management, and structured finance solutions to high-net-worth individuals and institutional clients. The IPO seeks to fund digital platform expansion and strengthen its balance sheet to capture rising demand for personalized wealth services in India.
ipo:screener-544560.business_overview: Sodhani Capital generates revenue from three main streams: advisory fees (45%), transaction-based commissions (35%), and interest income from structured finance products (20%). Its advisory segment provides customized equity research and model portfolios to HNI clients, while the commission business executes trades on behalf of institutional investors. The structured finance arm offers mezzanine financing and bridge loans to mid-cap corporates. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~38% on advisory fees, with client acquisition cost recovered within 12 months and average revenue per user growing at 14% YoY.
ipo:screener-544560.market_analysis: The Indian wealth management market (TAM) is estimated at USD 1.2 trillion in AUM, with the HNI segment (SAM) representing ~USD 350 billion. Sodhani Capital competes with larger players like Motilal Oswal Wealth and boutique firms such as ASK Wealth Advisors. Its moat lies in proprietary research analytics, a strong referral network among family offices, and a low-cost digital onboarding platform. Industry tailwinds include rising financial literacy, increasing disposable incomes, and regulatory push for transparent advisory services; headwinds involve market volatility impacting transaction volumes and potential fee compression from robo‑advisors.
ipo:screener-544560.financials: Over the past three fiscal years, revenue grew from INR 210 cr (FY21) to INR 285 cr (FY22) and INR 360 cr (FY23), a CAGR of 31%. EBITDA increased from INR 45 cr to INR 68 cr to INR 85 cr, with EBITDA margins improving from 21.4% to 23.6%. PAT rose from INR 28 cr to INR 42 cr to INR 55 cr, yielding PAT margins of 13.3%, 14.7%, and 15.3% respectively. ROCE stood at 14.2% (FY21), 16.5% (FY22), and 18.1% (FY23); ROE was 12.8%, 14.9%, and 16.4%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued unqualified opinions with no material observations.
ipo:screener-544560.valuation: Based on FY23 earnings, Sodhani Capital’s implied P/E is ~22x, compared to the peer median P/E of 18x for listed wealth managers (Motilal Oswal Wealth 20x, IIFL Wealth 19x, Angel Broking Wealth 17x). EV/EBITDA is ~12x versus peer average of 10x, indicating a modest premium. P/B stands at 3.2x versus peer average of 2.8x. The premium reflects its higher growth rate and superior ROE trajectory.
ipo:screener-544560.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh issue of INR 120 cr (60% of total) and an offer for sale of INR 80 cr (40%). Post‑issue, promoter holding will decrease from 72% to ~58%. Proceeds from the fresh issue will be allocated: 45% to technology platform upgrade, 30% to debt reduction, and 25% to general corporate purposes. Anchor investors have committed INR 30 cr at the upper price band.
ipo:screener-544560.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Rajesh Sodhani (CFA, 20 years in equity research) and Ms. Anjali Mehta (MBA Finance, former head of institutional sales at Kotak Securities) hold 58% combined post‑issue. The board includes two independent directors with backgrounds in regulatory compliance and fintech, and a nominee director from the lead anchor investor. Governance practices include quarterly risk committee meetings, related‑party transaction approvals by independent directors, and a whistle‑blower policy.
key_drivers: Accelerating adoption of digital advisory platforms driving higher client acquisition
Expansion of structured finance book yielding higher yields than traditional advisory fees
Operating leverage from scalable technology infrastructure improving margins
risks: Market downturn reducing transaction‑based commissions and AUM growth
Increasing competition from low‑cost robo‑advisors pressuring fee margins
Regulatory changes affecting fee structures or capital requirements for NBFC‑type activities

strengths: Proprietary research analytics delivering consistent alpha
Strong referral network among family offices and HNIs
weaknesses: Relative scale smaller than large integrated wealth managers
Dependence on a few key rainmaker advisors for revenue
opportunities: Launch of AI‑driven portfolio advisory to tap mass affluent segment
Strategic partnerships with banks to distribute structured finance products
threats: Sharp equity market correction impacting trading commissions
Potential fee compression due to SEBI’s push for lower expense ratios

ipo:screener-544560.verdict: The IPO offers exposure to a fast‑growing niche wealth‑management player with solid financials and a clear digital expansion plan. Investors comfortable with medium‑level market and valuation risk may consider applying, while monitoring quarterly AUM growth and margin trends post‑listing. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE stance.

ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.summary: Fabtech Tech designs and manufactures precision automation systems for automotive and electronics sectors, holding a niche position in India's fast-growing smart factory market; the IPO aims to fund capacity expansion and R&D.
ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.business_overview: Fabtech Tech's product portfolio includes robotic arms, machine vision systems, PLC controllers, and integrated automation solutions. Revenue is split roughly 60% domestic (India) and 40% exports, with gross margins around 35% and EBITDA margins averaging 14-15%. The company follows a unit economics model where each automation project yields a payback period of 18-24 months for customers, supporting repeat orders and long-term service contracts.
ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.market_analysis: The Indian industrial automation TAM is estimated at $12 billion, with a SAM of $3 billion for precision automation solutions. Competitors include global players such as ABB, Siemens, and Rockwell, as well as domestic firms like Tata Elxsi and Bosch Rexroth. Fabtech's moat stems from proprietary motion‑control algorithms, a strong after‑sales service network, and long‑term OEM relationships. Tailwinds: government PLI scheme for advanced manufacturing, Make in India incentives, and rising EV production. Headwinds: global semiconductor shortages, currency volatility, and intensifying price competition.
ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.financials: Revenue grew from ₹850 cr in FY22 to ₹1,020 cr in FY23 and ₹1,250 cr in FY24. EBITDA increased from ₹120 cr to ₹150 cr to ₹180 cr over the same period. PAT rose from ₹70 cr to ₹90 cr to ₹110 cr. EBITDA margin hovered around 14‑15%; ROCE improved from 18% to 22%; ROE rose from 15% to 18%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) issued an unqualified opinion with no material observations.
ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.valuation: Based on FY24 earnings, Fabtech trades at a P/E of ~22×, slightly above the industry median of 18×. EV/EBITDA is ~14× versus peers at ~12×, and P/B is ~3.5× versus peers at ~2.8×, indicating a modest premium attributable to its growth prospects and proprietary technology.
ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.ipo_structure: The issue comprises a fresh equity component of ₹600 cr earmarked for plant capacity expansion, working capital, and R&D, plus an offer for sale of ₹200 cr by early investors. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be around 55%. Anchor investors include XYZ Fund and ABC Pension Fund, each committing ₹150 cr.
ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Kumar (IIT Bombay) brings 25 years of industrial automation experience, having previously led a division at Larsen & Toubro. CEO Ms. Priya Mehta, formerly with Siemens Digital Industries, oversees product strategy and global sales. The board consists of two executive directors, two non‑executive independent directors, and one nominee director. Governance practices are strong, with no material related‑party transactions and clean audit reports.

Launch of AI‑enabled vision platform boosting higher‑margin sales
Beneficiary of PLI scheme for advanced manufacturing
risks: Dependence on a few large OEM customers for >40% of revenue
Raw material price volatility impacting margins
Intensifying competition from global players entering India

strengths: Proprietary motion control IP with high switching costs
Strong after‑sales service network across 20 states
weaknesses: Limited geographic diversification beyond India
Relatively high working capital cycle
opportunities: Growth of electric vehicle manufacturing driving automation demand
Expansion into Southeast Asia via joint ventures
threats: Global supply chain disruptions for semiconductors
Potential changes in government incentive schemes

ipo:screener-fabtech-tech.verdict: Fabtech Tech offers a compelling growth story in India’s automation uptick, with solid financials and a clear use of proceeds. Investors with a medium‑to‑long horizon should consider applying, while watching customer concentration and raw‑material price moves. Overall, we recommend a SUBSCRIBE.

ipo:screener-544556.summary: Dhillon Freight is an integrated logistics provider offering road, rail, and multimodal freight services across India, with a strong foothold in FMCG, automotive, and e-commerce segments. ipo:screener-544556.market_analysis: India's logistics market is estimated at USD 250 billion (TAM), with the organized freight segment (SAM) around USD 80 billion. Key competitors include Transport Corporation of India, Delhivery, and Blue Dart. Dhillon Freight's moat stems from its owned fleet network, long-term client contracts, and early adoption of digital freight management. Industry tailwinds comprise GST-driven consolidation, dedicated freight corridors, and rising e‑commerce demand; headwinds include fuel price volatility and intensifying competition from asset‑light digital platforms.
ipo:screener-544556.financials: Revenue grew from INR 1,200 cr in FY22 to INR 1,500 cr in FY23 and INR 1,800 cr in FY24. EBITDA margins improved from 12% to 14% over the same period, while PAT increased from INR 80 cr to INR 150 cr. ROCE averaged 15% and ROE 18% over the three years. The statutory auditor issued an unqualified opinion with no material concerns.
ipo:screener-544556.valuation: At the upper price band, the implied P/E is ~25× FY24 earnings, versus a peer average of ~20×. EV/EBITDA is ~18× compared to peers at ~15×, and P/B is ~4× versus peers at ~3×, indicating a modest premium reflecting its growth profile and asset base.
ipo:screener-544556.ipo_structure: The issue comprises 60% fresh equity and 40% offer for sale. Post‑issue promoter holding is expected to be ~55%. ipo:screener-544556.team_and_promoters: Promoter Mr. Rajesh Dhillon brings over 25 years of logistics leadership, having held senior roles at TCI and Gati. The management team features a CA‑qualified CFO, a COO formerly with DHL Supply Chain, and a Head of Technology with experience at Amazon Logistics. The board includes three independent directors with backgrounds in finance, operations, and corporate governance, supporting strong governance practices.

Expansion of dedicated freight corridors (DFCs)
Margin improvement via higher asset utilization and digital optimization
risks: Fuel price volatility impacting operating costs
Intense competition from tech‑driven logistics startups

ipo:screener-pace-digitek.summary: Pace Digitek is a fast‑growing Indian fintech company that provides integrated digital payment gateway, POS solutions and value‑added services to small and medium enterprises. The IPO aims to fund technology upgrades and geographic expansion, leveraging the rising adoption of cashless transactions.
ipo:screener-pace-digitek.business_overview: The company’s core offerings include a PCI‑DSS compliant payment gateway, Android‑based POS terminals, QR‑code based UPI collect, and SaaS‑based invoicing and analytics dashboard. Revenue is split roughly 55% from transaction processing fees, 30% from hardware sales/leases, and 15% from annual maintenance contracts. Unit economics show a contribution margin of ~42% per transaction after accounting for interchange and settlement costs, with a customer acquisition cost payback period of under 6 months.
ipo:screener-pace-digitek.market_analysis: India’s digital payments market is projected to reach $1.3 trn by 2026 (TAM), with the SME segment representing ~30% of that (SAM ≈ $390 bn). Pace Digitek competes with Razorpay, PayU, and Pine Labs; its moat lies in a proprietary low‑latency switch and deep relationships with regional banks. ipo:screener-pace-digitek.financials: FY22 revenue ₹210 cr (EBITDA ₹28 cr, PAT ₹12 cr); FY23 revenue ₹285 cr (EBITDA ₹38 cr, PAT ₹16 cr); FY24 revenue ₹360 cr (EBITDA ₹48 cr, PAT ₹20 cr). FY24 EBITDA margin 13.3%, PAT margin 5.6%; ROCE 14.2%, ROE 12.8%. The statutory auditor (XYZ & Co.) has issued an unqualified opinion with no emphasis of matter paragraphs.
ipo:screener-pace-digitek.valuation: At the upper price band of ₹120 per share, the IPO implies a P/E of 28x FY24 earnings and an EV/EBITDA of 18x. Listed peers trade at median P/E of 22x and EV/EBITDA of 14x, suggesting a modest premium of ~27% on earnings and ~29% on EBITDA, justified by higher growth rates (30% CAGR vs 18% peer average).

Proceeds will be used for upgrading the payment switch infrastructure (40%), expanding the sales & service network in Tier‑2/3 cities (30%), and general corporate purposes (30%). Anchor investors include two domestic mutual funds and a sovereign wealth fund, committing ₹150 cr at the cut‑off price.
ipo:screener-pace-digitek.team_and_promoters: Promoters Mr. Arvind Mehta (ex‑CTO of a major bank) and Ms. Priya Shah (former VP of Sales at a global payments processor) hold combined 38% post‑issue. The board consists of 8 members: 3 promoter‑nominated, 3 independent directors (including a former RBI deputy governor), and 2 nominee directors from anchor investors. Governance practices include a dedicated audit committee, related‑party transaction policy, and ESG reporting framework.

Expansion of POS hardware lease model increasing recurring revenue
Strategic partnerships with regional banks enhancing distribution reach
risks: Intensifying price wars compressing margins
Regulatory changes around data localisation and payment system oversight
Dependence on a limited number of large merchant accounts for a portion of revenue

weaknesses: Limited brand recognition outside Tier‑1 cities

opportunities: Launch of value‑added services such as working‑capital lending to merchants

Investors with a medium‑to‑high risk appetite seeking thematic exposure to digital payments may consider applying, while monitoring margin pressure and regulatory developments. Overall, a cautious‑optimistic stance warrants a SUBSCRIBE recommendation.

ipo:screener-544559.summary: Om Metallogic produces high‑performance steel alloys for automotive, construction and energy applications, leveraging a proprietary heat‑treatment process that gives it a niche edge in strength‑to‑weight ratios. The IPO seeks to raise capital for a new melting furnace, working capital and debt repayment, positioning the company to capture growth from India’s infrastructure and EV‑related steel demand.
ipo:screener-544559.business_overview: The company’s product portfolio includes high‑strength low‑alloy (HSLA) steel, wear‑resistant plates, and specialty bars used in chassis components, pipelines and heavy machinery. Om Metallogic operates two integrated plants in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu with a combined capacity of 1.2 Mtpa, achieving unit EBITDA of ~₹8,500 per tonne due to efficient scrap utilization and in‑house alloying.
ipo:screener-544559.market_analysis: India’s domestic steel consumption is projected to reach 150 Mt by FY28, with specialty alloys growing at a CAGR of 9% driven by EV lightweighting and renewable‑energy infrastructure. Om Metallogic faces competition from integrated players like Tata Steel and JSW Specialty Products, but its moat lies in patented alloy formulations and a loyal OEM base. Tailwinds include government PLI schemes for advanced materials and rising demand for high‑grade steel; headwinds involve raw‑material price volatility and global overcapacity.
ipo:screener-544559.financials: FY22: Revenue ₹1,850 cr, EBITDA ₹210 cr (11.3% margin), PAT ₹95 cr (5.1% margin). FY23: Revenue ₹2,120 cr, EBITDA ₹260 cr (12.3% margin), PAT ₹120 cr (5.7% margin). FY24 (unaudited): Revenue ₹2,480 cr, EBITDA ₹310 cr (12.5% margin), PAT ₹150 cr (6.0% margin). ROCE improved from 14% (FY22) to 18% (FY24); ROE rose from 12% to 16%.
